rolls.heading: Hawkmoon DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 263 conversation with @illPhysics. PvP backup S-Tier roll for duelling. For Hand Cannon connoisseurs - you'll need to keep hitting crits and keep a count of how many stacks you have for the last round in the mag. Seven will delete anything in PvP. Six will one tap anyone not in a super, and five will two tap any resilience. Balance out boosts to Handling, Stability and Range using the barrel and Grip. Then Rangefinder improves both your damage and aim assist dropoffs.|tags: pvp, pvp-god, pvp-duel

rolls.heading: Hawkmoon DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB): "For PvE Surplus or Killing Wind will depend on your build. A higher ability build will appreciate Surplus, a more solo run-n-gun build will appreciate Killing Wind. You could definitely opt for some of the other perks here too as nothing really screams PvE."|tags:pve,mkb

A Swift Verdict DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB PvE Podcast 25. PvE first-choice roll for end-game content. Sidearms are a... brave choice of Primary for endgame content. Handling and Stability could both do with some help from sight and mag, or the Combat Effect mags are always useful. If you're going to embrace CQC, then Threat Detector and Surrounded (with the Spec mod for +40% damage) are your obvious choices. Drops from the second (Hexahedron Room) encounter if you're looking to farm it. Recommended MW: Stability or Handling, with a Surrounded Spec mod.|tags: pve, pve-god, pve-minor, pve-endgame, controller, mkb

rolls.heading: A Swift Verdict DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S16] [MW: Range/Stability] "Sidearms are getting better due to the operating space of PvP being pushed slightly further out, via SMG / Slug pressure and pellet spread inconsistency. A Swift Verdict is a decent choice, stocked with Handling investment perks and varied playstyle options (Zen Moment/Rangefinder vs. Kill Clip, for instance). Disruption Break is nice for teamshots, I just don't see it being fully utilised on a Sidearm." tags:pvp

rolls.heading: Aberrant Action Perks and God Rolls
Abide the Return DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 91 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/50NPk7xF3Tt6X3DVucE9jU?si=44d2b7d44a5a4aec) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for sustained damage to Boss Spec enemies in end-game content. If you already have a solar boss damage sword, you can pass. If you want to use Abide the Return, then get a blade perk that increases damage and, for a guard perk, Swordmaster's Guard speeds up your heavy attack regeneration or Balanced Guard gives you a little bit of everything to help survive in CQC with a boss. The usual trait combination of Relentless Strikes, returning one ammo for everyth three light hits, and Whirlwind Blade for an escalating +3% bump to damage topping off at +30% for landing light hits makes for a decent, if not top-tier option for boss DPS phases. The Advanced Reflexes origin trait is quite useful in PvE - when activated by an enemy hit, it adds +20 charge rate for faster heavy attacks, together with a handy +20 Mobility, for 7.5s. Recommended MW: Impact, with a damage boosting (Boss or Taken Spec) mod. Tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Abyss Defiant Perks and God Rolls
Action Item Perks and God Rolls
Adhortative DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-Utility PvE-Champion PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE roll for piercing Barrier Champions in activities rated Very Hard, Hard or Normal difficulty. The updated Adhortative is an Adaptive pulse rifle which as a subfamily are currently rated C-Tier for endgame PvE by @TheAegisRelic Endgame Analysis spreadsheet in https://www.destiny2.science/ as "small step down from C tier pulse damage profile, but has barrier intrinsic". The frame intrinsic offers no bonuses beyond being "well-rounded, reliable and sturdy", as per the https://www.destiny2.science/ Destiny Data Compendium. The stats are on the low side by comparison with its peers - you can see the stats at https://destiny.report/w/3744058588/the-third-axiom and hopefully https://d2foundry.gg/w/3744058588 will show how weapon stats impact in-game performance when the site is back up and running. The recoil is a little left and a bit loose, so to tighten that up, look for a barrel perk that helps with Recoil Direction or, if you can handle it just go for extra handling, and then with the magazine perk go for Armor-Piercing Rounds for the additional ×1.05 shield damage, one of the other combat effect mags or something that helps improve magazine size or reload. This is, admittedly, a niche anti-Barrier roll but in the first column you could look for Attrition Orbs to spawn a power orb after ~26 hits within 5s of each other to proc armour mods or Shoot to Loot which lets you pick up remote ammo bricks or orbs from safety by shooting them, which also reloads all your weapons from reserves - both are always useful. In the second column, Disruption Break increases kinetic damage by ×1.5 for 5.5s after breaking an elemental shield or barrier, you should be able to quickly finish off Barrier Champions from a safe distance with a kinetic special weapon. The Nano-Munitions origin trait grants a stack up to ×7 for being within 15m of an ally, using a stack to refill 25% of your primary or secondary weapon mag when empty. If you're interested, "Adhortative" relates to the giving of advice, encouragement, or strong urging, such as saying "Okay everyone, let's wipe, rally and have another try". Recommended MW: Handling, with your preferred choice of mod to boost weapon stats, ammo generation, or elemental pickups for builds.|tags:PvE PvE-Utility PvE-Champion PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Adhortative DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "With such a similar pulse existing in PvP it comes down to perks. Forge’s Pledge gets access to Accurized and Steady Rounds, while Adhortative comes with High-Caliber and Ricochet Rounds. Forge’s Pledge comes with Zen Moment and Elemental Capacitor for big bonuses in stats, while Adhortative has Headseeker or Incandescent for better neutrality or some AoE. Depending on your preferred play style you might prefer one over the other, but I cannot emphasize this enough: both can work wonders." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Adjudicator God Rolls and Perk Pool
Admetus-D DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies episode 136 from 1 April 2025 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/6I828LxsIqdth4iIlSYRmG?si=8022df8ede664223) with @CourtProjects, @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-Boss PvE-MajorSpec PvE-Champion PvE-God PvE-EndGame M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE pick for piercing Barrier Champions in both general at-level or better, and end-game underleveled content, particularly with void builds. Admetus-D is a rare energy Häkke weapon - a void scout rifle with the High-Impact frame intrinsic of ×0.8 accuracy cone size and ×0.83 accuracy cone growth when you're stationary or moving less than 2.8m/s, for easier crits and hits. At base, by comparison with its peers, the stats are good - 65 Range equates to 80.88m ADS and 40.44m hipfire before damage dropoff, 29 Stability is ×1.1202 flinch resist at 100 Resilience, 30 Handling is 0.45s ready, 0.36s ADS and 0.44s stow speed, 35 Reload Speed is a 2.27s reload, Recoil Direction is 76, Zoom is 21, and magazine size is 14 (max 20). Handling is the stat that needs the most TLC, so that's the top choice for the barrel perks. Then, for a stick-it-in-your-vault for when the artifact mod is around, the combination of Armor-Piercing Rounds for ×1.05 damage to shields and barriers, together with Repulsor Brace for a void overshield for killing void debuffed targets and Withering Gaze to apply 15% weaken for an effective ×1.176 damage boost for 6s for your whole fireteam should make short work of Barrier Champions. If you're interested, Admetus means "untamed" and was the king of Pherae in Thessaly, Ancient Greece, who was saved by Apollo from his fated death when his wife Alcestis offered to die in his place. The Häkke Breach Armaments origin trait is situationally useful, passively boosting damage against vehicles (×1.15), constructs (×1.3) and stasis crystals (×1.45). Recommended MW: Reload Speed or Handling, with a Backup Mag mod or, when masterworked, Synergy to create Void Breaches or, when the artifact mod's around, Stunloader. Also, don't forget to check whether there's a useful Seasonal Fragile mod for the activity you're running.|tags:PvE PvE-Boss PvE-MajorSpec PvE-Champion PvE-God PvE-EndGame M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Admetus-D DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "When it comes to PvP these scouts need all the help they can get. And Withering Gaze is just that. An extra effect that anyone on your team can take advantage of. Outside of that, Lone Wolf and Keep Away both help boost your lower stats." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Adverse Possession IX DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Another weird choice for New Monarchy weapons to bring back, the Adverse Possession IX isn’t even the longest scout rifle New Monarchy had on offer. Overflow is going to be my main pick as it bypasses reloading entirely. Frenzy is ultimately going to be your best damage perk in column 4 so by all intents and purposes this scout is underwhelming compared to any other scout, even outside its archetype." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Adverse Possession IX DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Precision scouts not named Hung Jury SR4 with Boxed Breathing aren’t exactly meta. Even those named aren’t meta either, they’re just strong within their niche. Elemental Capacitor allows you to juice one of your stats and works best on Arc or Void (I would not imagine Strand being the best option), and Keep Away allows you to have tighter accuracy cones, but that still won’t make this gun competitive." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Age-Old Bond DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Range, Stability, Handling, Reload Speed. High-Impact Frame(360) Void Auto Rifle Primary Ammo.  "I am excited for this auto to pair with a void build using both Repulsor Brace and Destabilizing Rounds for a self buff and add clear. Golden Tricorn pairs well if you get Volatile from your subclass or just want a stronger weapon. Dragonfly in the third column will get beefed up with Golden Tricorn so I can see some utility outside of direct void explosions that could be usable all the way up to GM levels." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Age-Old Bond DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Stability. High-Impact Frame(360) Void Auto Rifle Primary Ammo.  "In PvP there are simply better options right now. Unlocking the patterns and getting them ready in the event of a buff would be wise, but quick killing weapons with good forgiveness coupled with Target Lock are the name of the game. Age-Old Bond kills quickly but does not have much in terms of forgiveness. Target Lock drops your 6 crit requirement down to 5 crit 1 body, not changing the time to kill, but making it ever so slightly more forgiving. With a bigger buff like Kill Clip you drop an entire shot needed to 4 crit 1 body. Not the most meta option." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Albedo Wing Perks and God Rolls
Albruna-D DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "For any PvE activity I will always try and prioritize recoil direction. Especially on special weapons or your main DPS weapon to make follow up shots easier. Any mag boosting perk will make the overall roll better. The usual suspects of triple tap, clown cartridge and field prep all make their appearance. Alongside our usual damage perks; firing line, frenzy and vorpal weapon. All of these damage perks are phenomenal, but use case specific. Firing line only works when in a full fireteam or raid, and you’d have to be within range for it to work. Frenzy only really works as long as you’re continuously taking damage or dealing it. Vorpal is the easiest to work with but also has the lowest damage output of all three." Recommended MW - Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Albruna-D DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "For PvP it is a decent zoom high-impact frame. You can make a choice between opening shot or snapshot if you crank up the handling high enough. Elemental capacitor will certainly help with that. Perpetual motion and moving target are also both incredible perks on snipers, one boosting low stats, the other adding forgiveness through aim assist." Recommended MW - Handling.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Anonymous Autumn DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies episode 124 from 19 November 2024 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/3n08KP410ui1TXoEdBmsvu?si=2badf5a8fcac408d) with @Saint_Kabr, @ImpetusAlways and @CourtProjects (PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE pick for add-clearing Minor enemies in  general, non-underleveled content, particularly with arc builds. If you've missed out on decent arc sidearms from previous seasons, Anonymous Autumn is a decent option. It does drop from Crucible activities with a 12×12 trait perk pool, so if you're after it, reset your Shaxx rank as many times as you're able before focusing. At base the stats are okay - 29 Range equates to 14.59m ADS and 12.16 hipfire, 60 Handling is 0.21s ready, 0.26s ADS and 0.31s stow speed, while the Reload Speed of 45 is a 1.63s reload. Range on sidearms is really compressed, so you can boost Handling, Stability (particularly for Controller) and reload with your barrel and magazine perks. If you didn't get a Brigand's Law with Voltshot, then Eddy Current in the first trait perk slot to speed up reload is a perfect pairing, synergising with the Arc verb toolkit, particularly if you enhance the weapon to unlock the Synergy mod to generate Ionic Traces. You might feel more immediate benefit from the One Quiet Moment origin trait for reloads, but if you can stay alive, you'll start to feel Field Tested as you get to the higher stacks. Recommended MW: Reload Speed, with a Backup Mag mod or, when enhanced, an Synergy mod to generate Ionic Traces.|tags:PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Anonymous Autumn DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Where Anonymous Autumn and TrueVanguard both find their homes is in the crucible. Range is going to be key as sidearms get shredded in distance by autos and SMGs. Pushing out your effective range can help someone backpedaling or just when you're above them. Lone Wolf, Zen Moment, and Rangefinder all improve the feel of this weapon in different ways, coupled with Kill Clip for explosive damage in your second engagement, Precision Instrument to shave off some crits required or even drop the TtK on lower resiliences would be good if you can reliably hit crits. Harmony really shines when swapping between weapons." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Appetence DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 92 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/2qpoxXfXzBXohHBrdQnHbd?si=aH9jqq3kRwOtwQoh2YhHDA) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for add clearing Minor spec enemies in end-game content. Shapeable. Appetence (which means "a natural craving, desire or affinity", seeing as you're asking) is the first legendary Stasis trace rifle. It has decent stats, and some interesting trait combinations. Take your pick of handling and/or stability and maybe a touch of recoil direction, if you need it to be a perfect 100, for the barrel perk. With an eye on Overflow as the support perk in the first trait column, Ionized Battery maximises the battery size. Enhanced Overflow gives you 229 in the battery, and 476 total ammo (so 247 in reserve) with no Reserve mods, up to 606 with three. Killing Tally is the best of the damage perks here, providing a 30% non-timed damage boost as long as you don't swap weapons. With Overflow topping up the battery, for just picking up an ammo brick, this could mean a pretty-much permanent bonus damage. The Dragon's Vengeance origin trait can be useful, although it only procs once your health bar turns red or an ally dies. You'll get a reload and 11s of a boost to range and either charge rate or handling. Recommended MW: Stability or Handling, with a damage boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Appetence DIM Voltron recommendation

rolls.heading: Astral Horizon Perks and God Rolls
Aureus Neutralizer Perks and God Rolls
Auric Disabler Perks and God Rolls
Aurora Dawn DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies episode 153 from 9 September 2025 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/6iEksMDGzT5euNuMAEqnVm?si=a9bf49987cdb441a) with @CourtProjects and @Saint_Kabr (PvE PvE-Boss PvE-BossSpec PvE-God PvE-EndGame M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE roll for CQC Boss damage in both general at-level or better, and end-game underleveled content. Aurora Dawn is a wave frame, so the heavy wave attack costs 4 ammo with an extra 1 for the optional targeted follow-up. At base, by comparison with its peers, the stats look good - Impact and Charge Rate are both highest in the subfamily, and they're improved further at Tier 4/5 when blade and guard perks are enhanced - see https://d2foundry.gg/w/2111625436 for how the weapon stats impact in-game performance. Base total ammo is 66 (max 77). Use the blade perk to maximise damage and Swordmaster's Guard guard perk improves Charge Rate by +10 for the heavy attack. Swords may not be the current meta for CQC boss damage, but if you want to tuck away a roll just in case, there's no first column trait perk to refund ammo, but Sharp Harvest returns 5% of special ammo reserves for 3 hits within ~1.5s (~2.0s when enhanced) or, if you're occasionally blocking against boss damage, Flash Counter emits a damaging Stasis blast in an 8m long cone in front of you that disorients enemies for 5s. For damage in the second column there's some viable options. Whirlwind Blade applies a stacking damage buff on hits within 3.5s of each other up to ×10 for ×1.3 damage. On a Transcendent subclass Elemental Honing grants an escalating stack of damage up to ×1.35 for 20s (23s enhanced) for each elemental damage type inflicted, which can be refreshed with a new damage type until you've run through all 5. For shorter damage windows, Binary Orbit provides a ×1.25 damage boost for 8.5s (10.5s enhanced) on any elemental kill of the opposite type. The Vanguard Vindication Origin Trait provides up to 3 stacks of ×0.98/0.96/0.94 damage reduction for 10s, for kills within 20s of using a class ability. Recommended MW: Impact, with your preferred choice of mod/enhanced mod to boost mag size, weapon stats, ammo regeneration, or elemental pickups for builds - and don't forget to check any available seasonal fragile mods.|tags:PvE PvE-Boss PvE-BossSpec PvE-God PvE-EndGame M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Aurvandil FR6 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast Versus Enemies episode 51 with @ImpetusAlways, @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5BpRdHN5pWyyKNLGsLB1Qh?si=4b9f4a74bf194ff2). First-choice PvE roll for stasis synergy and end-game content. The first kinetic slot high-impact fusion rifle - it needs handling and stability to improve gunfeel, but it might be worth hanging onto in case the archetype gets a little love. Fusion Rifle range is very constrained, so look for handling with your barrel, and Ionized or Enhanced Battery adds extra shots. Reconstruction then doubles your mag size, and bypasses any reload penalty and means that the top 3/4 of your magazine will now proc Chill Clip. The oversized magazine means you should also benefit from the Omolon origin perk stability boost. Recommended MW: Handling, Stability or Charge Time, with a damage-boosting (Minor, Major or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, Controller, M+KB|tags:1820235745

Elemental capacitor can make it feel super consistent on void, arc or stasis. Firmly planted will make the shot you do take incredibly consistent, void or not." Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

We have gotten a few new aggressive frame shotguns recently, especially within this DLC. The interesting thing about this shotgun is the inclusion of Nanotech Tracer Rockets pushes this shotgun a little over the edge compared to its brothers and sisters. You will want to increase Basso Ostinato’s handling to make swaps a lot less painful. Envious Assassin and Grave Robber are great options depending on what you’re using this shotgun for and with. If you are using Basso Ostinato as a DPS weapon or mob killer, then something like Trench Barrel or Destabilizing Rounds will be perfect with Envious Assassin. If you punch a lot you can pair Grave Robber with either of the punchy perks." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Basso Ostinato DIM Voltron PVP recommendation

However, Perpetual Motion does not stack up as well compared to Threat Detector, Perpetual Motion 2x is still not even as strong of a handling buff as Threat Detector 1x. You will want to crank up the handling as much as possible. rolls.heading: Battle Scar DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-Minor PvE-GroupAdd PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE roll for clearing groups of Minor adds in activities rated Normal, Hard and Very Hard difficulty. The reprised Battle Scar is a Lightweight pulse rifle which as a subfamily are currently rated C-Tier for endgame PvE by @TheAegisRelic Endgame Analysis spreadsheet in https://www.destiny2.science/ as "essentially just a worse high-impact auto". The frame intrinsic offers built-in anti-Overload, and +20 to the hidden Mobility stat and ×1.0625 base movement speed as per the https://www.destiny2.science/ Destiny Data Compendium. The stats are decent by comparison with its peers - you can see the stats at https://destiny.report/w/1525080480/battle-scar and hopefully https://d2foundry.gg/w/1525080480 will show how weapon stats impact in-game performance when the site is back up and running. The recoil is vertical but loose, so to tighten that up, look for a barrel perk that helps with Recoil Direction or, if you can handle it just go for extra handling, and then with the magazine perk go for something that helps improve magazine size or reload. For clearing groups of adds, in the second trait perk column Kinetic Tremors emits three 6m AoE Kinetic shockwaves after 11 hits (10 when enhanced) within 3s of each other, which are treated as weapon damage so inherit damage boosts. You can pair either Shoot to Loot, which lets you pick up remote ammo bricks or orbs from safety by shooting them, which also reloads all your weapons from reserves or Attrition Orbs spawns a power orb after ~28 hits within 5s of each other to proc armour mods - both have their range of effect extended by the kinetic shockwaves. The Veteran's Wisdom origin trait procs on a crit kill or 3 kills within 3s of each other to generate an 8m (9m when enhanced) AoE kinetic shockwave which inflicts Exhaust on enemies, or there's Field-Tested which starts subtly but if you can stay alive to get kills, you'll start to feel the gunfeel stat boosts as you get to the higher stacks. Recommended MW: Handling, with your preferred choice of mod to boost weapon stats, ammo generation, or elemental pickups for builds.|tags:PvE PvE-Minor PvE-GroupAdd PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller

They are usable with damage perks. Battle Scar gets Multikill Clip for damage and Eye of the Storm for consistency. Depending on what you value you could swing it either way." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Better Devils Perks and God Rolls
Bite of the Fox DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Adamsdown_Boy - information as at v8.0.0.6 PvE / PvE-Boss / PvE-BossSpec / PvE-EndGame / PvE-God / M+KB / Controller): PvE pick for killing boss-tier Subjugators. As per @mossy_max (https://x.com/mossy_max/status/1804893174965338387) - boss-tier Subjugators have a 20% damage reduction to all non-matching damage, going up to 30% DR to all damage after they "screech", which counts as a shield in-game and reacts to shield-based trait perks. Bite of the Fox - which can still be obtained at the time of writing from legacy Iron Banner engram focussing. First, Arrowhead Brake to help with recoil, or Chambered Compensator or Polygonal Rifling will also to help land consistent crits). Then Appended Mag which, with a Backup Mag mod, gets you to 5 in the mag (again, if your drop one, Extended Mag works too). Envious Assassin in the first trait perk column overflows the mag up to 15, or Rapid Hit helps lands crits and boosts reload speed, as a second choice. Finally, Under-Over provides +50% damage against the boss Subjugator "shield". The Skuling Wolf origin trait is pointless outside of PvP, unfortunetely. Recommended MW: Stability, with a Backup Mag mod

Push out the range as far as you can with barrel, mag and Masterwork. Rangefinder is the best trait to both push range out further, and constrain bullet spread. rolls.heading: Bottom Dollar DIM Voltron PVP recommendation

It's incredibly, incredibly unlikely you'll ever pin down a personal 5/5 roll (due to there being 12 potential perks... in each column), so I'd recommend going to town with one that ticks a few boxes for you and seeing if you can put in work. Explosive Rounds are incredibly strong on harder-hitting weapons, coupling with High-Cal due to the massive flinch they give off allowing you to challenge less-confident Snipers (and occasionally LFRs). The achievable range on Bottom Dollar is crazy, and if it matches your playstyle, you'll do very well with it." tags:pvp

rolls.heading: Boudica-C DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB PvE Podcast 32. PvE first-choice roll for endgame content. Sidearms aren't first choice for end-game activities but, if you're forced to use one there because of an anti-Champion mod, the you do have some options here. Range is highly constrained (maximising it only gets you another 0.7m when ADS), so you'll get more value out of Handling or Stability from your barrel, and any of the combat effect mods (Armor-Piercing, High Caliber or Riochet Rounds) will work. For end-game stuff, you'll have to get close up, so use that to your advantage with Threat Detector and then Surrounded provides a hefty 46% damage boots with the spec mod (once that's rolled in to the perk by Bungie, go for a damage-boosting mod or Backup Mag). Recommended MW: Stability or Handling, with a Surrounded Spec mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, Controller, MKB

Brigand's Law DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S18] [MW: Stability] "Brigand's Law is fantastic. The Blunderbuss styling on a pocket-sized sidearm, the perk package including Threat Detector or Hip-Fire Grip, Enhanced Surrounded Spec going up to a 47% damage buff, then Voltshot which is simply phenomenal on this weapon type. Always invest MASSIVELY into Stability on Sidearms - Range quite simply does not matter for damage falloff. Is it Forbearance, or Trinity Ghoul? No. But hopefully, if we move back to a world where Primary weapons matter again, this is definitely in my backpack and ready for action." tags:pve|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Brya's Love DIM Voltron PVE recommendation

Brya's Love has some incredible perk combinations I would love to try out. Destabilizing Rounds on void has some incredible utility. Precision Instrument will probably take the cake as the next Vorpal Weapon being able to scale up damage with ease. Pair that with Rapid Hit to really get the most for your precision hits. Another great perk to craft would be Shoot to Loot to get your ammo and orbs from a distance. Not being required to chase after orbs is a pretty big benefit." Recommended MW: Handling or Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 261. PvE backup roll. Shapeable. The Extrovert origin perk is most useful for CQC (within 15m), so probably not going to be that useful on a Rocket Launcher. Not the best subtype for PvP, but if you must use it, boost the Blast Radius as high as possible with barrel, mag and Masterwork, add Tracking, so you have a chance of hitting something while you're in the air, and Chain Reaction may get you an extra kill if someone's are tickled and then finished off by a team-mate exploding. BxR-55 Battler DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 280 with @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvP pick for duelling. Shapeable. Just great stats across the board, plus the unique hipfire boost from the frame make the Battler a top choice for PvP. You're always likely to be taking damage, so the Hot Swap origin perk will be useful too. Out-of-the-box, this has the same TTK as a lightweight - 0.87s with 7 crits and 1 body, or an accuracy-adjusted TTK in 0.93s (6c, 3b), as at v6.2.5. It does have wonky recoil, which Arrowhead Brake sorts out completely, but extra stability plus the 20 Zoom also makes it controllable. Ricochet Rounds, is the reliable mag perk of choice. Then, for duelling, Perpetual Motion boosts your gunfeel stats and Eye of the Storm makes crits easier to hit in a firefight. Recommended MW: Handling or Range, with a Freehand Grip, Quick Access Sling, Sprint Grip or Targeting Adjuster mod depending on your preference.|tags: PvP, PvP-Duel, MKB, Controller

rolls.heading: BxR-55 Battler DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB PvE Podcast 26. PvE first-choice roll for end-game content. This is a stat monster - use the barrel to improve Recoil Direction, Handling and Stability, and boost stats further with the mag. Perpetual Motions will take your gunfeel stats over 90 when active and then Blunt Execution Rounds pairs well with the various melee options available as at Season 17 v4.1.5.2 for a 500% damage boost for one burst. Recommended MW: Handling or Reload Speed, with a damage boosting (Minor, Major or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: pve, pve-god, pve-minor, pve-endgame, controller, mkb

rolls.heading: Canis Major DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S15] (PvE): [MW: Velocity] "Canis Major is the new king of 150RPMs. Whereas Impulse Amplifier was recently limited to just Memory Interdict (which has truly abysmal Velocity), it now being on Canis Major flat-out gives it the highest DPS potential for the slot. No other HGL comes within sniffing distance of this thing's max Velocity. And then they gave it Vorpal Weapon too. Best burst damage roll; [Hard Launch / Spike Grenades / Impulse Ampifier / Vorpal Weapon]. Its only drawback is the lack of an auto-loading option. If you're looking for an auto-reloader, Love and Death will be your best shot.|tags:god-pve

rolls.heading: Cantata-57 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Cantata-57 feels good, and that’s at base. Everything else just builds this gun up to be a beast in PvE content; rivaling even Fatebringer. Ideally you’d want rapid hit and vorpal to cover your majors and minors, but timed payload is a great PvE perk similar to explosive payload. The delay in PvE is negligible and you get nothing but benefits. Rapid hit only works if the bullet portion hits crits but with the aim assist and PvE hit boxes you shouldn’t have a horrible time getting rapid hit going." Recommended MW: Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Cantata-57 DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "Having eye of the storm and rangefinder or opening shot is a combo we haven’t had since Season of Opulence [...]. Eye of the storm and opening shot give you better accuracy for almost your entire gunfight. Whereas rangefinder allows you to comfortably compete with Palindrome. You could also opt for rapid hit to make your consecutive crits easier to hit, or the newly buffed hip-fire grip should you want Under Your Skin levels of spicy. Cantata-57 also completely power crept Annual Skate out of the water by allowing it incredible neutral perks and access to timed payload. Cantata-57 has set itself up to be a really dominant threat to the “top 3” hand cannons." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Cataclysmic God Rolls and Perk Pool
Cataphract GL3 Perks and God Rolls
Chain of Command DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Adamsdown_Boy - information as at v8.0.0.6 PvE / PvE-Boss / PvE-BossSpec / PvE-EndGame / PvE-God / M+KB / Controller): PvE pick for killing boss-tier Subjugators. As per @mossy_max (https://x.com/mossy_max/status/1804893174965338387) - boss-tier Subjugators have a 20% damage reduction to all non-matching damage, going up to 30% DR to all damage after they "screech", which counts as a shield in-game and reacts to shield-based trait perks. Chain of Command was the ritual chase weapon back in Season 19. The fixed roll does offer Adaptive Munitions, but it wouldn't be a first choice to use as there's no reloading or magazine extending trait perk. As it's stasis, Adaptive Munitions in the second trait column will work against Strand Subjugators, and you can use Osmosis in the first column to change the damage type to work against Stasis ones for a total damage boost of around +45% after 15 hits. Remember Adaptive Munitions only works against non-matching shield types, otherwise you're left with a less impressive +25% damage boost from bypassing the damage resistence on Stasis Subjugators. One Quiet Moment is the most useful of the origin traits, to speed up reload. Recommended MW: Stability, with a Backup Mag mod

rolls.heading: Chattering Bone Perks and God Rolls
Chivalric Fire DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / Controller / M+KB): "Our full ritual playlist weapon is an interesting one. Caster frame swords have not been strong since their introduction. So Bungie is either testing us to see if they should buff caster frames or trying to get more hands on feedback. I feel like caster frames are a tough one to balance. Give them too much damage and they become an extremely safe pick in everyday play. Lower the ammo requirements and you will inadvertently buff their DPS as you’ll have more opportunities to use the heavy attack. Lower their charge time to counterbalance either of those changes and we’re back to square one. Chivalric Fire is a great neutral sword that works best on Void subclasses, but you can utilize either orb generation or volatility to great success without committing to Void." Recommended MW: Impact.|tags:pve,controller,mkb

rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 91 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/50NPk7xF3Tt6X3DVucE9jU?si=44d2b7d44a5a4aec) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for add-clear of Minor Spec enemies in end-game content. Like most world-drop weapons, Cold Front has pretty poor stats - including a stability of just 7, although (excluding Immortal, which is a stat monster), they're average for the frame subtype. Use the barrel and mag perks to help out with this and then the SOFA (Stats/ One For All) combination of trait perks to round out the stats further and add a chunky 35% bonus damage. Contingency Plan DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 275 conversation with @DefyFly. First choice PvP roll for kill-chaining. You don't need extra range, so choose between Fluted Barrel for Handling or Polygonal Rifling for pure Stability (or maybe Arrowhead Brake if you find the Recoil Direction needs taming), and add more Stability with Steady Rounds in the mag. Kill Clip, in the second trait column, reduces the TTK to a competitive 0.7s (3 crits, 1 body), as at Season 18 v6.2.0.6, and you can pair this with Tunnel Vision which procs on the same condition and makes your follow-up kills easier. Recommended MW: Stability or Handling, with a Radar Tuner (to help you stay aware of your surroundings when hardscoping), Quick Access Sling or Sprint Grip mod depending on your preference.|tags: PvP, PvP-Chaining, PvP-God, MKB, Controller

rolls.heading: Coronach-22 DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 56 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/6hjWp0NaachsNymnN22FDL) with @ImpetusAlways, @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects. (??? ???): First-choice PvE roll for solar synergy and Minor add-clear in general content. The Suros weapon for season 20 is the Coronach (named after a funeral dirge sung, or played on the bagpipes, in Scotland and Ireland), a solar Adaptive auto rifle with the lowish stats you'd expect from a World drop. The Suros origin trait can help fill in a little Handling and flinch resistance after a reload although it's more commonly thought of as being more PvP-focussed. If you didn't pick up an Ammit with Incandescent from Witch Queen, this gives you access to a similar (if unenhanced) set of perks. Arrowhead Brake as your barrel perk takes Recoil Direction to 100 and adds Handling for snappiness. To fill in the other gunfeel stats, Flared Magwell boosts Stability and Reload Speed, or Light Mag boosts Range and Reload Speed in the mag. As the support perk, in the first trait column, Feeding Frenzy boosts reload speed further after kills - your solar ignition kills from Incandescent will add to the stacks, particularly when paired with Ember of Ashes. Recommended MW: Stability or Handling, with a damage-boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-God, Controller, M+KB

rolls.heading: Crowning Duologue Perks and God Rolls
Cruel Mercy Perks and God Rolls
Cruoris FR4 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies episode 136 from 1 April 2025 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/6I828LxsIqdth4iIlSYRmG?si=8022df8ede664223) with @CourtProjects, @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-God PvE-EndGame M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE pick for clearing tougher adds in both general at-level or better, and end-game underleveled content, particularly with a double special loadout. Cruoris FR4 is a rare Aggressive Frame fusion rifle that shoots three horizontal bursts of four bolts in a widening spread - think of how the Bastion exotic works. At base, by comparison with its peers, the stats are decent - standard 667ms charge time, 37 Range equates to 21.09m ADS and 16.22m hipfire before damage dropoff, 36 Stability is ×1.11.80 flinch resist at 100 Resilience, 34 Handling is 0.45s ready, 0.35s ADS and 0.35s stow speed, 31 Reload Speed is a 2.27s reload, Recoil Direction is 55, Zoom is 15, and magazine size is 6 (max 8). With 0/1/2/3 reserves mods, total ammo is 17/20/23/24 (max 25). Handling is the stat that needs the most TLC, so that's the top choice for the barrel perks. As the battery perk, boost the battery size or use Accelerated Coils to reduce charge time. If you're running double special then, after a kill with another weapon, Discord in the first trait perk column gives you 7s of free ammo on a kill as well as reducing both ADS animation and accuracy cone size to ×0.75. Pair that with One For All in the second column for a ×1.3 damage boost for hitting three enemies, and you can cook anything within 15m or so. If you're interested, Cruoris is the genitive singular form of the Latin word for blood or blood clot, "cruor". The Omolon Fluid Dynamics is a useful origin trait, boosting stability and reload speed in the top half of a battery (which should be easy with Discord). Recommended MW: Reload Speed or Handling, with a Backup Mag mod or, when masterworked, Synergy to create Ionic Traces or, when the artifact mod's around, Stunloader. Also, don't forget to check whether there's a useful Seasonal Fragile mod for the activity you're running.|tags:PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-God PvE-EndGame M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Cruoris FR4 DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "These frames were never good in PvP. I wouldn't recommend it at all. Kickstart helps give you more damage per shot to secure the kill in 4 bolts instead of 5, but that's still hard with an uncontrollable horizontal spread." Recommended MW: Range or Charge Time.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

Roar of the Bear coming from Iron Banner is a rocket, which has better use cases. Fixed Odds coming from the Duality dungeon and feels impossible to farm for. Dark Decider DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Reload. Rapid-Fire(720) Arc Auto Rifle Primary Ammo.  "While I like Dark Decider coming back and more Veist-Rapid Fire Frame autos is never a bad thing. They are incredibly fun to use. It harps back to Vanilla when I used Hollow Earth nearly every day. A better version in this economy? Heck yeah. Golden Tricorn with an Arc subclass will give you damage that scales up to almost GM level activities. Dragonfly gives you some great add clear potential with Veist Stinger to keep up the shooting, pair that with Subsistence and you can almost non-stop firing. Titans Rejoice a legendary Sweet Business that has ‘splosions!" |tags:pve,mkb,controller

Ricochet Rounds gives you some great stability before Dynamic Sway Reduction kicks in." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Darkest Before DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-Minor PvE-GroupAdd PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE roll for clearing groups of Minor adds in activities rated Normal difficulty, particularly with Solar builds. The reprised Darkest Before is a Rapid-Fire pulse rifle which as a subfamily are currently rated C-Tier for endgame PvE by @TheAegisRelic Endgame Analysis spreadsheet in https://www.destiny2.science/ as "essentially just a worse high-impact auto". The frame intrinsic offers built-in anti-Overload, and a ×0.8 reload animation multiplier when starting a reload with an empty mag as per the https://www.destiny2.science/ Destiny Data Compendium. The stats are pretty much best in class by comparison with its peers - you can see the stats at https://destiny.report/w/859869931/nullify and hopefully https://d2foundry.gg/w/859869931 will show how weapon stats impact in-game performance when the site is back up and running. The recoil is left and a little loose, so to tighten that up, look for a barrel perk that helps with Recoil Direction or, if you can handle it, just go for extra Handling, and then with the magazine perk go for something that helps improve magazine size or reload. For solar build synergy, there's the fan-favourite trait perk combinations of Heal Clip to provide ×2 Solar Cure (140 HP), and ×1 Cure on allies within 15m, when you finish a reload within ~5.3s (~5.5s when enhanced) after a kill, or Attrition Orbs spawns a power orb after 26 hits within 5s of each other to proc armour mods together with Incandescent which spreads +30 Solar Scorch stacks within 4m on a red bar Minor add kill (+40 stacks within 8m for orange and yellow bars, +45 when enhanced). The scorch damage counting as weapon damage so can inherit any weapon damage bonuses and proc weapon effects like Attrition Orbs. All of the scorch stacks are increased by ×1.5 with Ember of Ashes. The Crossing Over origin trait is a little weird, providing extra range and handling in the top half of the mag, and a small damage boost in the bottom starting at ×1.015 at 50% and ×1.03 with your penultimate and last shot. If you're interested, "Darkest Before" is a play on the classic proverb, "It is always darkest before the dawn" - darkness being the absolute peak of a crisis, just before a breakthrough or resolution, when light appears. Recommended MW: Handling, with your preferred choice of mod to boost weapon stats, ammo generation, or elemental pickups for builds.|tags:PvE PvE-Minor PvE-GroupAdd PvE-God M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Darkest Before DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Darkest Before in PvP lost a little bit of arc shenanigans, but gained a better perk pool. Elemental Capacitor and Headseeker will make for an extremely consistent or snappy pulse on void or solar, respectively. Kill Clip and Heal Clip together make for a lethal and synergistic build. I don’t foresee Darkest Before becoming the king of pulses, but definitely a top contender for those rapid-fires." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Dead Weight DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Rapid-fire shotguns do not fare so well in PvP compared to PvE. Full choke is still mandatory to secure the best possibility for a two-tap kill. Accurized rounds pushes out the fall off so you’d still be able to do that solid 1 damage at 3m and beyond. Perpetual motion brings up the already healthy handling. Where Dead Weight falls off hard is in the final column. Not having access to some of the top tier perks like opening shot or even elemental capacitor to max out the range adds to the downfall of this shotgun. Just don’t use rapid-fire shotguns in PvP." Recommended MW - Handling.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Deadpan Delivery DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 78 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5KOQQ9QtvSEhRVb4vjeO3B?si=8c4784e02b87424e) with @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects. First-choice PvE pick for burst damage against Major or Boss Spec enemies in end-game content. Decent stats in comparison with its peers. So if you're a Lethal Current Hunter in the market for a shotgun to pair with your melee, Deadpan Delivery is a very decent choice. For a One-Two Punch roll, start with Full Choke, to make hitting all your pellets a little easier. Tactical Mag increases the magazine to a maximum of 5 (which is the same as Appended and Extended Mags). Then, as the first trait column support perk, there's no Grave Robber unfortunately, but Overflow doubles your magazine size for picking up ammo, which gives you 10 shots before you'll have to reload (unless you pickup another ammo brick). Finally, One-Two Punch in the second column as the signature perk boosts your powered melee damage by a hefty 40%. You probably won't get much use out of the Nadir Focus Origin Trait, which adds range and accuracy on repeated hits, but it is nice to have anyway. Recommended MW: Handling, with a damage-boosting (Major, Boss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Deadpan Delivery DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "I'm not going to lie. I got excited by this shotgun for one mere fact. Slickdraw. I know it's not everyone's cup of tea, but it has been mine for the past season. I have been slowly collecting Slickdraw weapons and changing crafted weapons to utilize Slickdraw. Now I know Slickdraw reduces your auto-aim cone size; it just means you have to be a bit more accurate when aiming. You can achieve a similar effect of Slickdraw by going with Arc Elemental Capacitor, Barrel Shroud, a Handling MW, and Quick Access Sling for 96 handling. This gives you a ready speed of 0.20s and an aim down sights speed of 0.23s. However just having Slickdraw drops you to the same ready speed and drops a hundredth of a second off of aim down sights. This means you can invest every other stat point into range. Bungie knew what they were doing when bringing in this weapon as Deadpan Delivery has the second lowest range stat of all Aggressive Frame shotguns even losing to the Uncommon Ded Nemoris II." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: Death Adder DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S15] (PvE): [MW: Stability / Reload Speed] "I'm unashamedly part of the Death Adder fan-club. While it shares a model with Recluse, its actually an entirely different SMG. Best used within its own Dragonfly niche, you'll want something to bolster its Reload Speed, or, if on console, address its (not great) Stability. Those come in the forms of Subsistence, Feeding Frenzy, and Dynamic Sway Reduction. Outside of those, fight your instincts and value Stability/Reload over Range -- I'd even consider Steady Rounds of all things. Great SMG, but it makes you work for its potential."|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Dire Promise DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 263 conversation with @illPhysics. PvP first-choice S-Tier roll for duelling. Originally a 150 Lightweight subtype that was rolled into the 140 Adaptive subtype, and suffers a little from being the only 13 Zoom HC in the sandbox. Stats make it very consistent feel - aim for stability and handling, to improve gunfeel, rather than range in barrel and mag. Snapshot Sights still (at S17) gets you into ADS in an engagement faster, and Rangefinder improves both your damage and aim assist dropoffs. Recommended MW: Handling, with a Targeting Adjuster mod if you're more passive, Sprint Grip/Quick Access Sling mod if you're more aggressive, or even give Rader Tuner mod a spin.|tags: pvp, pvp-god, pvp-duel

rolls.heading: Disparity DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Disparity is probably the best weapon to come out of the season. A craftable aggressive burst pulse rifle that looks good, feels good, has a fantastic sight [...]. There are so many great perks to go for but the base will all be the same. Arrowhead to have near-perfect recoil direction. Flared magwell or tactical mag for a bump in reload / stability with the latter having a slight bump in magazine. Rapid hit is my preferred third column option but outlaw is easier and immediate. No need to build up stacks, just headshot and done. Frenzy is the immediate choice for a “no thoughts, head empty” workhorse weapon. Headstone and desperado are both fantastic for their respective builds. The former gives you slow, cover, and stasis synergy, while the latter gives you that Rambo effect turning your pulse into a mini machine gun." Recommended MW - Stability.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Disparity DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "Taking that fantastic base and transitioning it over to the crucible is easy. Most of the weapon stays the same. Arrowhead and rapid hit are the foundation. Steady rounds for more stability, or accurized rounds for even more range is your preference. In the last column you have three separate ways you can go. Kill clip is the easiest to use and helps make the second kill easier as you won’t need as many critical hits. Headseeker is my pick for overall neutral play. Desperado is great for kill chaining in 6v6 but I wouldn’t use it in 3v3." Recommended MW - Range or Handling.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Disparity DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB PvE Podcast 44. Shapeable. First-choice PvE roll for synergy with Stasis subclasses. The Ambush origin trait is probably more for PvP than PvE, with the extra range, handling and 10% boost to damage for a few moments after combat starts, but Hakke Breach Armaments, with the additional damage against stasis crystals is the choice to break crystals for a dragonfly-like burst of damage. This is the only current(as at v6.3.0.2) me aggressive frame pulse rifle in the kinetic slot, so it's worth hanging on a good roll. Correct the wonky recoil direction with Arrowhead Brake, or Fluted Barrel makes it feel snappier and more stable. In the mag, Flared Magwell or Tactical Mag are the best options, adding more stability and reload speed. To match up with Headstone, Rapid Hit and Outlaw both proc on the same condition to reward consistent crits. Recommended MW: Handling or Reload Speed, with a damage-boosting (Minor, Major or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-God, Controller, M+KB

rolls.heading: Double-Edged Answer DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 93 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/0uYGSQqlyX1faCyATCDuno?si=IMSCRgTRQr-_THJdjxFMIQ) with @ImpetusAlways and @Saint_Kabr. First-choice PvE pick for burst damage against Major Spec enemies, or Boss spec enemies with short damage windows in end-game content. Double-Edged Answer has awful base stats and a huge perk pool so, if you are looking for a roll, reset your Vanguard reputation a few times first. Jagged Edge provides the most damage as a Blade perk, but decimates your ammo, so Honed Edge and Tempered Edge might be attractive alternatives. For this roll, you'll be looking to block damage, so a Guard perk like Burst Guard or Balanced Guard works best. This is the only sword dropping in S23 with the combination of Flash Counter and Counterattack - get in close to a tanky enemy to bait them into a melee/slam attack, and then the combination of weaken and bonus damage provides +80% damage for 2s, and +20% for the next two, which should be enough time to put a serious dent in their health bar. Nadir Focus could be an interesting mini-Eager Edge - each light attack increases lunge distance, if you want some fun. Recommended MW: Impact, with a damage boosting (Boss, Major or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

The in-air accuracy nerf hurt all weapons, but I think sidearms were one weapon type that got hit the hardest. I think swashbuckler is the best option for the last column as melee kills in cqc are common and going into the next fight with a large damage boost will make your sidearm hit like a hand cannon." Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Edge Transit Perks and God Rolls
Eleatic Principle DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Adamsdown_Boy - information as at v8.0.0.6 PvE / PvE-Boss / PvE-BossSpec / PvE-EndGame / PvE-God / M+KB / Controller): PvE pick for killing boss-tier Subjugators. As per @mossy_max (https://x.com/mossy_max/status/1804893174965338387) - boss-tier Subjugators have a 20% damage reduction to all non-matching damage, going up to 30% DR to all damage after they "screech", which counts as a shield in-game and reacts to shield-based trait perks. Eleatic Principle is a shapeable LMG from Witch Queen, but although you can craft it to your liking, it's not the strongest LMG to use for this use case being shorter ranged and more difficult to control than adaptives. First, Arrowhead Brake to help with recoil (if you just drop one, Chambered Compensator or Polygonal Rifling will also to help land consistent crits). Then Extended Mag with a Backup Mag mod gets you to 115 shots in the mag or again, if you drop one, Appended Mag with Backup Mag gets you to 110. In the first trait perk column, there isn't a huge choice - Ensemble boosts reload speed in a fireteam, or Eddy Current is the best on offer if you're solo. In the second, Adaptive Munitions buffs damage to non-matching Subjugator "shields" for a total damage boost of around +45% after 15 hits (+73% when enhanced). The Head Rush origin trait mostly pointless - unless you make of habit of crouching and then standing up to shoot.  Recommended MW: Stability, with a Backup Mag mod

Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: Enyo-D DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "Where I’ve heard the most chatter around Enyo-D is in the Crucible. People are calling it the Antiope-D 2.0 or the kinetic Shayura’s Wrath. I think both are true, but the Enyo-D still has some shortcomings when it comes to comparing against the both. Enyo-D does not have kill clip. Multikill clip is fine and good, but the immediate 33% damage bonus is incredible on any precision frame smg. Enyo-D also has less zoom than both. The zoom field has been condensed, but the 16 zoom smgs will still be some of the best out of the gate. Steady hands also brings up one of Enyo-D’s weakest stats and makes it feel incredibly fluid. Keep an eye out for this one." Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

Improve Handling and Stability with your barrel and mag. The perks are mainly PvP orientated, but Surrounded, particularly with the Spec mod, adds a hefty 40% bonus CQC damage, and Feeding Frenzy helps with keeping the perk active. rolls.heading: Evening SI4 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @Saint_Kabr, @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-Minor PvE-GroupAdd PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE roll for clearing groups of Minor adds in general at-level or better content, particularly with Solar builds. Evening SI4 is an adaptive burst sidearm, which are rated B-Tier for endgame PvE by @TheAegisRelic tiering spreadsheet https://linktr.ee/TheAegisRelic as an decent sidearm, interchangeable with other frame subfamilies. The frame intrinsic fires a 3-round burst and offers no bonuses beyond "being well-rounded and reliable" as per the https://www.destiny2.science/ Destiny Data Compendium. The stats are solid by comparison with its peers - hopefully when the website's back https://d2foundry.gg/w/1763361847 will show how the weapon stats impact in-game performance. First priority with the barrel perk is Handling, and then with the magazine perk look for something that helps improve magazine size or reload. For clearing groups of adds in general content with a solar build, there's the fan favourite trait perk combination of Heal Clip to provide ×2 Solar Cure (140 HP), and ×1 Cure on allies within 15m, when you finish a reload within ~5.3s (~5.5s when enhanced) after a kill, together with Incandescent to spread +30 Solar Scorch stacks within 4m on a red bar Minor add kill (+40 stacks within 8m for orange and yellow bars, +45 enhanced), with scorch damage counting as weapon damage so can proc weapon traits like Heal Clip, and inherit any weapon damage bonuses. For solar subclasses, all of the scorch stacks are increased by ×1.5 with Ember of Ashes. For this roll, you might be lucky and get the rare Accelerated Assault origin trait, which passively refills 10% of the magazine, and boosts damage by ×1.05 for getting 3 hits within a 1.6s window. Otherwise, Omolon Fluid Dynamics boosts Stability by up to +20 Reload Speed by up to +30 in the top half of the mag, decreasing linearly until the mag's at 50%. Of less interest is Vanguard Vindication which provides up to ×3 stacks that reduces incoming damage at max to ×0.94 for a refreshable 10s (12.5s when enhanced), on kills within 20s of using a class ability. Recommended MW: Handling, with your preferred choice of mod to boost weapon stats, ammo generation, or elemental pickups for builds - and don't forget to check any available seasonal fragile mods.|tags:PvE PvE-Minor PvE-GroupAdd PvE-God M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Evening SI4 DIM Voltron recommendation

Recommended MW: Reload, Range.|tags:pve,god-pve

Exile's Curse DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S16] [MW: Charge Time] "Slideshot/Kickstart is a really fun combo that allows you to completely snipe people with High-Impact Fusions. HIFs are in a strange spot since the Fusion Rifle re-work, as they're pretty much outclassed by every other archetype due to how long the charge time is now. Invest in Charge Time > Range > Stability in order of priority. If the Adept version were to return, Adept Charge Time could come in clutch here. For now, treat it as a fairly stationary snipe / Super shut-down option, unless there's a second rework that addresses HIFs." tags:pvp

rolls.heading: Extraordinary Rendition DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S15] (PvE / PvP): [MW: Stability / Reload / Range] "A personal favourite. For PvE, invest in; [Stability / Reload / Subsistence/Overflow / Rampage/Frenzy]. For PvP, invest in; [Surplus / Multikill Clip]. After IKELOS_SMG, this is likely the best 750RPM - and it definitely sounds like it. Subsistence is insanely strong on SMGs post-Primary changes, and Frenzy gives the perfect reload buff that SMGs direly need in PvE. In PvP, this is a strong contender, and up there as one of the best Legendary options, after Multimach in the same slot."|tags:god-pve,god-pvp

False Promises DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "False Promises is a returning auto rifle from yesteryear. It's a High-Impact frame with decent handling but pretty poor stability. There's not a lot you can do to improve that outside of directly investing or giving up a perk like Feeding Frenzy for Dynamic Sway Reduction. I wouldn't advise it unless you have a really hard time controlling the recoil. Cascade Point and Headseeker provide alternating utility through increasing rpm without a damage penalty or Stasis synergy. Depending on your build I'd personally go with Cascade Point because it feels more fun." Recommended MW: Reload Speed.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: False Promises DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Over in PvP False Promises has a bit more of a leg up. High damage per shot paired with a high range stat will cover most maps outside of scout rifle ranges. Cascade Point shows up here again as the firing delay is a whopping 40% dropping the time to kill from 0.83s to 0.50s. The requirements for a kill don't change at 6c but being far faster will catch people by surprise." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Father's Sins DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Father’s Sins is craftable but doesn’t offer as much. Focused Fury is great for choosing when to get your damage and being able to reload and triple tap into a bigger mag is a huge boon. But damage phases are moving to more mobile sets, so “stand and DPS” is becoming less common. Even still having one in the event a “stand and DPS” phase comes back would be nice to keep in your back pocket.". Recommended MW - Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Father's Sins DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Lower zoom snipers did just get a small buff to their aim assist cones, and Father’s Sins is a 35 zoom sniper. That being said, it doesn’t immediately make them good, nor does it make them bad. Most people have shifted towards higher zooms, namely 45 and 50 zooms. The two things that can bump up Father’s Sins in my book is the ease of getting one and crafting it to your liking, as well as having access to under pressure and opening shot. With a higher base aim assist than the likes of Occluded Finality and Shephard’s Watch it could see some use being the bullet bending sniper. Time will tell, but it wouldn’t hurt to keep one around.". Recommended MW - Handling.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Faustus Decline DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies episode 148 from 5 August 2025 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/3S0TObWihybmZdCLM6wIUw?si=cf08fccbb9d44fa7) with @CourtProjects, @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-God PvE-EndGame M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE roll for clearing Minor adds in both general at-level or better, and end-game underleveled content, particularly with a Stasis build. Faustus isn't a bad sidearm - the Lightweight frame boosts mobility - now the hidden Athletics stat - for better base movement speed and jump height. But at the end of the day it's still a primary sidearm, all of which need a bit of attention, with mainly PvP trait perks. At base, by comparison with its peers, the stats are good - d2foundry.gg calculates that 25 Range equates to 12.00m hipfire and 14.40m when ADS before damage falloff, 42 Stability is ×1.0550 flinch resist at base, 68 Handling is 0.24s ready, 0.19s ADS and 0.30s stow speed, and 50 Reload Speed is a 1.58s reload. Ammo Generation is 50, Recoil Direction is 95, and Zoom is 12. Bumping up Handling with the barrel perk will make the gunfeel noticibly snappier, but on controller you might want to look at Stability too. For the magazine perk, Alloy Magor Flared Magwell are reliable options for boosting gunfeel stats, or one of the combat effect options adds utility. For Stasis builds, you have the Rimestealer and Headstone combination to build Frost Armor stacks on killing frozen enemies or shattering Stasis crystals, which you can generate on crit kills. The Bray Legacy Origin Trait is solid in PvE, generating around 0.2% energy per hit for whichever ability is closest to a full charge. If you're interested, "Dr Faustus" is an Elizabethan tragedy by Christopher Marlowe, based on German stories about a scholar who sells his soul to the devil in exchange for magical power. Recommended MW: Handling or Stability, with your preferred choice of mod/enhanced mod to boost weapon stats, ammo regeneration, or elemental pickups for builds - and don't forget to check any available seasonal fragile mods.|tags:PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-God PvE-EndGame M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Fel Taradiddle DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Whispering Slab has some decent competition. [...] You can easily hit the draw time floor of 540 with a couple different combinations and archer’s tempo does drop that even further. Pair that draw time with damage perks like adrenaline junkie or one for all. Or you can go the “double stagger” route with explosive head when overload bows come back as an artifact mod (there are few things more satisfying than infinite staggering a champion).". Recommended MW - Draw Time.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Fel Taradiddle DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "In PvP most people would opt for the higher damage archetype, especially now that Under Your Skin can have an almost immediate draw time. But Under Your Skin is also energy, so you have a kinetic option, again if you don’t have a Whispering Slab. The same perks can work in PvP as they do in PvE. Archer’s tempo for faster follow up shots and explosive head for the double flinch can mess up your opponents as they try to combat you.". Recommended MW - Draw Time.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

Fioritura-59 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast Versus Enemies episode 51 with @ImpetusAlways, @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5BpRdHN5pWyyKNLGsLB1Qh?si=4b9f4a74bf194ff2). First-choice PvE roll for void subclasses in general content. Decent stats by comparison with its peers but, at the end of the day, this is still a sidearm looking for an in-game role. Spec into stability with your barrel, and High-Cal Rounds for extra flinch, or Armor-Piercing Rounds (you're likely to be in a crowd in CQC, so APR can be useful to damage more than one enemy at a time), or Ricochet Rounds or Flared Magwell will add additional stability to help control the recoil. Pugilist can help recharge your melee to add a void verb to your target to proc Repulsor Brace on a kill and generate a void overshield to help tank damage in a crowd. Recommended MW: Stability or Handling, with a damage-boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, Controller, M+KB

rolls.heading: Fioritura-59 DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "Rapid-fire frame sidearms are the biggest Uno Reverse card for shotgun rushers. Spray them down and you don’t need a lot of range to do so. These weapons excel in cqc but also live and die by their consistency. Fioritura-59 has great lethality perks and will serve well in a very aggressive play style but will be hard countered by anyone walking backwards." Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Fire and Forget DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Other than Disparity, this is probably the best weapon to grind for this season. [...] Fire and Forget also being stasis allows you to go big damage with Silence and Squall and font of might. The aggressive burst linear fusions are definitely best in slot. Fire and Forget is an incredible gift from the Bungie gods. I would highly recommend bringing the recoil direction up to 100 with arrowhead brake. That way you can solely focus on fighting flinch. You can go a couple different routes with magazine and masterwork, but I prefer mag dumping and better burst dps with accelerated coils and charge time MW. Field prep is best for pure damage, but headstone and vorpal are probably what I will be going for. That way I can have some decent damage and crowd control. Chill clip is interesting and certainly viable, but I don’t know how useful it will really be in practice." Recommended MW - Charge Time.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Fire and Forget DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "In terms of using fire and forget in PvP. It’s kind of a mixed bag. It can definitely feel good and has some forgiveness you don’t really find on heavy weapons. I like aggressive bursts for PvP but I don’t find myself reaching for them often. Maybe a crafted version will change that. Go for as low of a charge time with as high handling and as much range you can fit into this wonderfully sexy weapon. Rangefinder will help keep aim assist at range, and high-impact reserves will help if you miss part of the burst." Recommended MW - Charge Time.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Firefright DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S18] [MW: Any] "Firefright takes the Scathelocke AR frame, so feels familiar, and comfortable. Craftable, and sporting new-wave perks that have had some lukewarm reception. Firefright is a reliable workhorse Auto that I'd spec highly into Stability/Handling, namely with perks like Threat Detector/Hip Fire Grip, then E-Cap, or Surrounded if you feel you can make use of the damage perk." tags:pve|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Firefright DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Similar to a lot of weapons this season, Firefright suffers from its frame, not its perks. Threat detector and elemental capacitor both are incredible on any weapon and adagio on a precision frame feels really fun to use. Because of vanilla D2 and the usage of Uriel’s Gift and other precision frame auto rifles, the current day auto rifles have the worst optimal and body shot TtK, resulting in lukewarm results at best, and at worst, not an enjoyable experience." Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Fixed Odds DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "High-Impact machine guns in PvP are not the first choice, and really not even the second or third choice either. Their TtK is competitive, but their forgiveness and ease of use is not. Arrowhead brake really cleans up the recoil and will make follow up shots a lot easier to hit. Pair that with accurized rounds to extend your engagement range. Finally, because of the nature of heavy ammo in PvP, you’ll be happy to grab a roll with under pressure for the added accuracy and stability. Best of luck to anyone who uses this." Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Folded Root Perks and God Rolls
Forced Memorializer Perks and God Rolls
Forensic Nightmare DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Precision frame submachine guns are in a weird spot. Having higher damage but lower stats across the boards combined with a pitiful reload creates an awful feeling weapon. You can counter this with perpetual motion to boost some of the stats or grave robber to straight up bypass the reload, but you still have an uphill battle to fight with the gun versus using Funnelweb or similar that need a lot less to feel good." Recommended MW: Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Forensic Nightmare DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Forensic Nightmare has a really nice scope, but the zoom is a let down. Shayura’s Wrath is just an upgrade and most people have one in their inventory. Especially now with the alacrity origin trait. The only benefit to Forensic Nightmare over Shayura’s Wrath is the fact it is craftable, for those of us (like myself) who haven’t gotten a good Shayura’s Wrath to use. Kill clip and perpetual motion or slideways both have merit against guardians, and being a stasis submachine gun does open up your loadout to have more viable combinations." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Forge's Pledge DIM Voltron PVP recommendation

I feel players can put in some good work with this, though may need to adjust their playstyle due to the somewhat reserved kill potential." tags:pvp

rolls.heading: Fortissimo-11 DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Much like Reckless Endangerment, I do think Fortissimo-11 will find better success in the crucible. With access to two of the best PvP perks it will easily find a home as a legendary contender to Chaperone, or a Heritage alternative. Threat detector and opening shot alone are enough to make it competitive. Add in some niche builds like adrenaline junkie and demolitionist for easy one shot body kills, or some more passive perks like moving target and perpetual motion to bring up the low aim assist and better the feel.". Recommended MW - Handling or Range.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Fortunate Stare Perks and God Rolls

Fractethyst DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S16] [MW: Range/Handling] "Pellet shotguns are fairly uniform in perk recommendations. Full Choke, Accurized, Slideshot/Quickdraw and Opening Shot. Maybe Assault Mag if you're finding you have to follow-up at range more often than not. If you're full-on aping and sliding then substitute Full Choke for Smallbore or Corkscrew. Copy/paste for the next 50 identical pellet-spread shotties." tags:god-pvp

rolls.heading: Fractethyst DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by Destiny Massive Breakdowns Podcast 228. PvE first choice roll. Recommended MW: Reload.

rolls.heading: Friction Fire DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 264 conversation with @pattycakesGG. PvP first-choice S-Tier roll for kill-chaining. Not obtainable as at S17, unfortunately, but even after the Zoom nerf, it's still very good. Precision frame, so recoil control is easier as the direction is pretty much vertical, and the 16 Zoom constrains bullet spread too. Push out range and zoom as far as you can with your barrel, mag and Masterwork. Killing Wind adds useful stat boosts after a kill, and Rampage is probably the most useful second trait, to help out with chaining kills. Masterwork: Range, with a Sprint Grip or Quick Access Sling, if you're playing aggressively, or a Targeting Adjustor for more passive playstyles.|tags: pvp, pvp-god, pvp-chain, controller, mkb

rolls.heading: Friction Fire DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S15] (PvE): [MW: Reload Speed / Stability] "If 600RPMs were to receive a PvE buff in future, Friction Fire would be a good weapon to have in your back pocket. Subsistence is incredibly strong post-Primary ammo changes, Threat Detector is always a top-tier pick for SMGs, and FF can roll with Rampage for easy ad clearance, Wellspring for ability boosting, and Vorpal if we're likely to see Anti-Barrier SMG again in future. It does struggle with Reload Speed, so invest in here, with the second priority being Stability, as this one's a bit bouncy."|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Frontier's Cry DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "I think it’s leaps and bounds better than Survivor’s Epitaph but still not as good as our old and faithful Trust. With access to rapid hit and kill clip on a shallower loot pool than Survivor’s Epitaph you just have better luck getting one to drop. That roll will serve you well in all facets of the game where a 180 hand cannon isn’t an active detriment (I won’t yell at you in a raid for using it, but I cannot vouch for everyone). There isn’t too much more to say on Frontier’s Cry as it’s a pretty self explanatory precision hand cannon that just falls into a PvE meta that won’t appreciate it.". Recommended MW - Stability.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Frontier's Cry DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "The choice of either kill clip or iron reach to really maximize lethality or consistency, respectfully, is a really solid selling point. Fully investing into range with full bore, accurized rounds, iron reach, and range MW puts you right into 34m as a precision hand cannon. Literally tying Crisis Inverted with a similar all range build. Trust on its best day had explosive payload but less range to work with. While I won’t recommend doing a full range investment as you lower other stats it is incredible to see a precision frame hand cannon hang with the more meta hand cannons.". Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Frozen Orbit DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S15] (PvP): [MW: Handling] "This is probably the best-feeling Sniper in the game when it has high-Handling rolls. At 75 stock Recoil Direction, its near-vertical out of the gate. Arrowhead takes this to 100, more noticeable on Console. You have a lot of options with Frozen Orbit -- Surplus + Snapshot is stupidly fast. No Distractions + Opening Shot is a fan favourite for ensuring calculated headshots. Vorpal actually isn't needed, as you should one-shot headshot almost all Supers. High Impact Reserves if you're a body-shot bandit. The perk pool sounds like it would have been a great PvE option, but the mag size simply isn't there -- stick with Uzume."|tags:god-pvp
