rolls.heading: Geodetic HSm DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 78 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5KOQQ9QtvSEhRVb4vjeO3B?si=8c4784e02b87424e) with @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects. First-choice PvE pick for add clearing Minor Spec enemies in end-game content. Geodetic (which means relating to the branch of applied mathematics concerned with the size and shape of the Earth) is a bit of a curious beast - having +2 Impact compared to other legendary swords (excluding Bequest) and +27 Charge Rate (again, excepting Bequest). If you're looking for an add-clear weapon, you might want to to look at Blade perks that boost your ammo capacity - one hit is likely to kill even in end-game content. Again, you're probably not looking to use the charged attack that frequently, so Burst Guard could be more useful to survive in CQC. This is the first sword that offers the trait combination of Repulsor Brace in the first column as the support perk and Destabilizing Rounds in the second as the signature perk, continuously generating a Void overshield and applying volatility to carve your way through adds. The Nadir Focus Origin Trait boosts lunge distance - a bit like an Eager Edge lite that's permanently up, which could be useful in the Crota's End Abyss encounter. Recommended MW: Impact, with a damage-boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-God, PvE-EndGame, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Gnawing Hunger Perks and God Rolls
Greasy Luck Perks and God Rolls
Gunnora’s Axe Perks and God Rolls
Hailing Confusion DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-Minor PvE-GroupAdd PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE roll for clearing groups of Minor adds in activities rated Normal difficulty, particularly with Stasis builds. Hailing Confusion returns as an Adaptive pulse rifle which as a subfamily are currently rated C-Tier for endgame PvE by @TheAegisRelic Endgame Analysis spreadsheet in https://www.destiny2.science/ as "small step down from C tier pulse damage profile, but has barrier intrinsic". The frame intrinsic offers no bonuses beyond "being well-rounded and reliable" as per the https://www.destiny2.science/ Destiny Data Compendium. The stats are decent by comparison with its peers - you can see them at https://destiny.report/w/4236134153/hailing-confusion and hopefully https://d2foundry.gg/w/4236134153 will show how weapon stats impact in-game performance when the site is back up and running. The recoil is left and loose, so to tighten that up, look for a barrel perk that helps with Recoil Direction or, if you can handle it just go for extra handling, and then with the magazine perk go for any of the combat effect mags or something that helps improve magazine size or reload. For Stasis synergy and clearing groups of adds in general content, in the first trait perk column Rimestealer generates ×1 stack (×2 stacks when enhanced) of Stasis Frost Armor on shattering a Stasis crystal or killing a frozen enemy, or Headstone generates a Stasis crystal on a crit kill. In the second column, Crystalline Corpsebloom procs on a kill to provide a 4s buff - kills with the buff active generate a Stasis seeker which travels 3m before spawning a medium Stasis crystal (large when enhanced). The Stasis crystals are weapon-generated entities so can inherit damage buffs, and proc Stasis subclass fragments when shattered for AoE damage. The Winterized Gear origin trait provides ×3 stacks after 12s out of combat - each stack decays after 5s in combat providing a gradually reducing boost to Stability, Handling and Reload Speed. If you're interested, the name reflect the harsh, disorienting nature of Europa's freezing storms as well as Clovis Bray's calculated military philosophy. Recommended MW: Handling, with your preferred choice of mod to boost weapon stats, ammo generation, or elemental pickups for builds.|tags:PvE PvE-Minor PvE-GroupAdd PvE-God M+KB Controller

Good feeling gun, puts in a lot of work in both modes, with some nice recoil and perks like Surplus/Quickdraw that help in general combat."|tags:pvp,pve

rolls.heading: Hand in Hand DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 56 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/6hjWp0NaachsNymnN22FDL) with @ImpetusAlways, @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects. First-choice PvE roll for sustained damage to Bosses and Majors in end-game content. The reprised Hand in Hand is the only non-endgame arc aggressive shotgun currently (as at v7.0.0.6) in game but, as usual for a world drop, the stats are on the low side - the New Devrim Kay VIII Foundry Field Tested origin perk should help, but it takes a long time to proc the higher tiers. Building towards One-Two Punch as your signature perk, Full Choke aids pellet spread, as you'll need to hit your target with all of them. Then Tactical Mag adds an extra extra shot (and a bonuses to reload speed and stability which admittedly, you'll not need so much). As your support perk in the first trait column, Slideshot is normally regarded as a PvP-flavoured perk but bypasses the reload to keep the magazine full for One-Two Punch in the second trait column. Recommended MW: Handling, with a damage-boosting (Major, Boss or Taken Spec) mod|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-God, PvE-EndGame, Controller, M+KB

rolls.heading: High Tyrant Perks and God Rolls
Hollow Denial DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Adamsdown_Boy - information as at v8.0.0.6 PvE / PvE-Boss / PvE-BossSpec / PvE-EndGame / PvE-God / M+KB / Controller): PvE pick for killing boss-tier Subjugators. As per @mossy_max (https://x.com/mossy_max/status/1804893174965338387) - boss-tier Subjugators have a 20% damage reduction to all non-matching damage, going up to 30% DR to all damage after they "screech", which counts as a shield in-game and reacts to shield-based trait perks. If you do have a Hollow Denial from Season 17, it's shapeable, so you can craft exactly the roll to help. First, a barrel that helps with stability, to keep your reticule steady will help land consistent crits. Then, as a Backup Mag mod alone gets you to the maximum of 107 shots, Light Battery or Tactical Battery boost reload speed. Adaptive Munitions increases damage to non-matching Subjugator "shields" by for a total damage boost of around +45% after 15 hits (+73% when enhanced). If you can get some Killing Tally kills in advance, you'll have an additional +30% damage (so, +125% total damage with enhanced Adaptive Munitions) until you stow or reload. You won't get too much use out of the Extrovert perk with this set-up unfortunately. Recommended MW: Stability, with a Backup Mag mod

rolls.heading: Horror Story DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 83 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/4ISDC0TSrLmfEyPuI9oU2m?si=9fbd5a7cf0bc4f07) with @ImpetusAlways, @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects. (First-choice PvE pick for Stasis synergy and add-clearing Minor Spec enemies in general content. Horror Story has returned and become a stasis precision auto-rifle - the first of its subtype. The stats are unexceptional, as you might expect from an event weapon but there are some trait combinations to save it. Recoil direction doesn't really matter on a precision-frame weapon, and stability is less important with a more reliable recoil direction, so you can choose your pick of Handling, Range and Reload Speed from your barrel and mag perks. Enlightened Action will get you pretty close to maxed out Handling and Reload at 5 stacks, or Demolitionist is difficult to turn down when it shows up in the first trait column as the support perk. In the second as the signature perk, Headstone is the main Stasis synergy trait, giving you crystals to work with your fragment, or burst and get even more grenade energy back with Demolitionist. Both origin traits are more PvP-orientated, but Nadir Focus's extra range and accuracy for free is a nice little bonus. Recommended MW: Handling or Reload Speed, with a damage boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: IKELOS_SR_v1.0.3 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Adamsdown_Boy - information as at v8.0.0.6 PvE / PvE-Boss / PvE-BossSpec / PvE-EndGame / PvE-God / M+KB / Controller): PvE pick for killing boss-tier Subjugators. As per @mossy_max (https://x.com/mossy_max/status/1804893174965338387) - boss-tier Subjugators have a 20% damage reduction to all non-matching damage, going up to 30% DR to all damage after they "screech", which counts as a shield in-game and reacts to shield-based trait perks. The Ilkelos Sniper rifle - still obtainable from the Seraph Shield mission - is shapeable, so you can craft exactly the roll to help. First, Arrowhead Brake to help with recoil (if you just drop one, Chambered Compensator or Polygonal Rifling will also to help land consistent crits). Then, as a Backup Mag mod gets you to the max of 7 shots, Seraph Rounds for all the benefits of High-Caliber, Armor-Piercing and Ricihet Rounds, or Steady Rounds for more recoil control. Enhanced Overflow in the first trait perk column overflows the mag to 15, or Fourth Times the charm will provide 10 crit hits without a reload. Finally, Under-Over provides +50% damage against the boss Subjugator "shield". Unfortunately, you won't see any benefot from the Rasputin's Arsenal origin trait as you're not actually breaking any shields. Recommended MW: Stability, with a Backup Mag mod

rolls.heading: Ill Omen Perks and God Rolls
Imperative DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Most of us already have. Workhorse PvE scout. If this is your first season playing destiny either as a new light or a returning player, Imperative has one great redeeming factor; damage. Imperative belongs to the high-impact archetype of scouts giving it the slowest firing rate but the highest damage. Pairing that with more damage through Adrenaline Junkie, some AoE damage through Kinetic Tremors or Explosive Payload. All of these will be happily paired with Rapid Hit for the bonuses to the low stability and reload speed. Subsistence and Keep Away both mitigate the low reload speed by bypassing reload entirely or speeding it up, respectively. I’d still recommend Rapid Hit over both as missing a shot is extremely unforgiving with the slower rate of fire. Precision hits are going to be the goal, why not make follow up shot easier while we’re at it." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Imperative DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Most of us already have. Workhorse PvE scout. If this is your first season playing destiny either as a new light or a returning player, Imperative has one great redeeming factor; damage. Imperative belongs to the high-impact archetype of scouts giving it the slowest firing rate but the highest damage. Pairing that with more damage through Adrenaline Junkie, some AoE damage through Kinetic Tremors or Explosive Payload. All of these will be happily paired with Rapid Hit for the bonuses to the low stability and reload speed. Subsistence and Keep Away both mitigate the low reload speed by bypassing reload entirely or speeding it up, respectively. I’d still recommend Rapid Hit over both as missing a shot is extremely unforgiving with the slower rate of fire. Precision hits are going to be the goal, why not make follow up shot easier while we’re at it." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Imperial Decree Perks and God Rolls
Imperial Needle DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S15] (PvE): [MW: Draw Time / Reload Speed] "Imperial Needle is the Void Bow. If you need a Void Bow for Overload Champions, this is the only Legendary one. Lightweight Frames typically don't have much advantage over Precision Frames, given that there's a "limit" for how low you can get Draw Time, but this will still steer you right. Most perks will work on this, Swashbuckler is pretty great for range variation, Quickdraw/Opening Shot if you're just wanting a Champion stopper, and Frenzy is great if you're playing mid-range. Truthfully, this is more of a PvP Bow, but we'll take the slot fillers where we can."|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Indebted Kindness God Rolls and Perk Pool
Infinite Paths 8 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-Minor PvE-SingleAdd PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE roll for clearing individual Minor adds or tougher enemies in activities rated Normal difficulty, particularly with Arc subclasses. The reprised Infinite Paths 8 is a Lightweight pulse rifle which as a subfamily are currently rated D-Tier for endgame PvE by @TheAegisRelic Endgame Analysis spreadsheet in https://www.destiny2.science/ as "essentially just a worse high-impact auto". The frame intrinsic offers built-in anti-Overload, and +20 to the hidden Mobility stat and ×1.0625 base movement speed as per the https://www.destiny2.science/ Destiny Data Compendium. The stats are on the low side by comparison with its peers - you can see the stats at https://destiny.report/w/3176697588/infinite-paths-8 hopefully https://d2foundry.gg/w/3176697588 will show how weapon stats impact in-game performance when the site is back up and running. The recoil is right and quite loose, so to tighten that up, look for a barrel perk that helps with Recoil Direction or, if you can handle it just go for extra handling, and then with the magazine perk go for something that helps improve magazine size or reload. For single target damage, synergising with arc builds, in the first trait perk column, Trickle Charge refills 10% of the magazine on receiving a stack of Arc Bolt Charge, and 20% when you discharge with a lightning bolt on a target, or Supercharged Magazine refills 5% of the magazine every 1.5s while you're Amplified or, after you sprint when Amplified to gain Speed Booster this accelerates to 5% every 0.25s while the buff is active, overflowing the magazine to up to ×2 capacity. Alternatively, for your neutral game, Demolitionist generates chunk grenade ability energy on kills and an instant magazine refill on grenade use. In the second column, for damage, Rolling Storm generates ×1 stack of Arc Bolt Charge (×2 when Amplified) on a kill, to trigger a lightning bolt at ×10 stacks for both target and AoE damage. The Radiolaria Transposer origin trait is useful for clearing adds - three Minors or one Major or Boss enemy kill will result in a 3m AoE explosion on death, leaving a damaging pool of radiolaria for a few seconds. The damage is treated as weapon damage so inherits damage boosts. If you're interested, "Infinite Paths," refers to the Infinite Forest Vex simulation engine on Mercury, while the "8" references Lost Prophecy, Verse 8 - the specific algorithm or riddle that Brother Vance gave us to forge the weapon back in Curse of Osiris. Recommended MW: Handling, with your preferred choice of mod to boost weapon stats, ammo generation, or elemental pickups for builds.|tags:PvE PvE-Minor PvE-SingleAdd PvE-God M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Infinite Paths 8 DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Mercules904 notes (not great PvP): "High aim assist, reload speed, and decent stability all combine to make this gun pretty decent when used correctly, but sadly it resides firmly outside of the current competitive meta due to the high precision accuracy needed to achieve the optimal TtK, and comparative weakness to Rapid-Fire's within the same engagement ranges. I definitely recommend trying to get one, but mostly to put in the vault until such a time as the non-Aggressive pulses get some of their range back (or Lightweights get a damage buff)."

rolls.heading: Interference VI DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S16] [MW: Reload] "Interference VI was the heavy of choice for the World's First Solo Atheon by VoteforShifu - if that's not enough to make it worth using, I don't know what is. Full Court is great in (specific) circumstances, going up to a 25% explosive damage buff when firing from 50m(!) away or more, meaning you're ideally firing at a very stationary, very large target. Spike Grenades are a +50% impact damage buff, and with Backup Mag, Clown Cartridge + Demolitionist, there's a real chance of you being able to fire 18 times in a row without reloading or stowing your weapon. Very strong contender should Heavy GLs get some time in the limelight again." tags:god-pve

The good news is that Irukandji is Stasis and Father’s Sins is Void so they won’t compete. If you have the adept version you can have incredibly high stats, 100 handling and 100 aim assist. rolls.heading: Jararaca-3sr DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast Versus Enemies episode 51 with @ImpetusAlways, @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5BpRdHN5pWyyKNLGsLB1Qh?si=4b9f4a74bf194ff2). First-choice PvE roll for stasis subclasses in end-game content. Even nerfed, the Veist Stinger origin trait will still be useful occasionally. Jararaca has poor stats, unfortunately, by comparison with other rapid-fire and Veist rapid-fire scout rifles, but it does offer Stasis synergy. First, ou'll need Arrowhead Brake to sort out the recoil direction. Any of the combat effect rounds will help - if you keep firing until the end of the magazine, the frame perk will help reloads, so you can live without a reload speed-boosting magazine perk, especially with Rapid Hit or Perpetual Motion in the first trait column. Finally, for Stasis synergy, in the second trait column Headstone's crystals work with your aspects and fragments. Recommended MW: Stability, with a damage-boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, Controller, M+KB

rolls.heading: Jararaca-3sr DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "This is just a stasis Contingency Plan. Which I am all here for. I love my Contingency Plan. You do have to bring up almost all Jararaca’s low stats. Hammer-forged for range or smallbore for some stability. Scouts generally don’t need range but it also doesn’t hurt to maintain your aim assist at those longer engagements. Arrowhead for recoil control as the 57 recoil direction is pretty low. Rapid hit is incredibly strong and kill clip is just the bee’s knees." Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Judgment of Kelgorath DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Let me be perfectly honest and say I do not like glaives in PvP. I understand their appeal and their usefulness. I just don’t vibe with them. I do know that immovable object and tilting at windmills are fantastic if you use your shield constantly (why wouldn’t you), Unstoppable force is the perk on glaives to maximize their effectiveness once you do get the shield up." Recommended MW - Shield Duration.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Judgment of Kelgorath DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB PvE Podcast 44. First-choice PvE roll for general content. Shapeable. The Ambush origin trait is probably more for PvP than PvE, with the extra range, handling and 10% boost to damage for a few moments after combat starts but will be more use for a glaive than Hakke Breach Armaments. JoK is a new subtype for Season 19 - a slower, but harder hitting slashy shooty stick than we're used to. You're going to be in CQC, where projectile speed (governed by range) isn't quite as important, so the shield can be quite useful to survive in a crowd. Use your haft to improve charge time and shield duration, and take the extra shot from Appended Mag (Extended Mag gives you no more shots so isn't worth it). In the first trait column, Overflow on a Special or Power weapon is just so good - more than double your magazine size and it bypasses the low reload speed just for picking up a brick! Close to Melee was just built for glaives - projectile kills improve melee damage by 30%, and more kills with either projectile or melee extend the timer. Proc and go have fun. MW: Range, with a damage-boosting (Minor, Major or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-God, Controller, M+KB

Jagged Edge and Swordmaster’s Guard will give you the best damage and most often heavy attacks, if you prefer different play styles with swords either using them as your main add clearing weapon through Incandescent or as less of a boss killer you might want to use Burst Guard or Enduring Guard for your build." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Last Rite DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Range, Stability, Handling, Reload Speed. Aggressive Frame(120) Kinetic Scout Rifle Primary Ammo.  "Last Rite is very much a solid Ritual Weapon. It doesn’t exist as an incredibly strong scout, but it does come with a solid set of perks. Reconstruction and Focused Fury do allow you to use a legendary 120 RPM scout rifle in PvE without it being Dead Man’s Tale. I cannot think of many situations where that’s necessary, nor really a need to use a 120 RPM scout. This does break up the need for an exotic as both Long Arm and Last Rite are legendary and therefore allow some loadout variation. However, the lack of specific perks like Rapid Hit, Explosive Payload, Kill Clip, or even Kinetic Tremors would bump this scout way up into usage. It’s certainly one of the scouts of all time." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

Rapid Hit would bump Last Rite up for me, but Reconstruction could work. I would not use this for hip firing but as a classic scout I wouldn’t be upset with a teammate using this." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Legato-11 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation

rolls.heading: Legato-11 DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Legato-11 really gets to spread its wings in the crucible. Closing Time and Lone Wolf are incredibly strong together. If you prefer Killing Wind you can pair that with Closing Time for a nice speed boost after one kill. Incandescent and Heal Clip are both solid options as slugs get one round and you can pick up another pretty quick for the reload. Offhand Strike is niche but very powerful in the right hands (or left)." Recommended MW: Range or Handling.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Lethal Abundance Perks and God Rolls
Lethophobia DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 92 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/2qpoxXfXzBXohHBrdQnHbd?si=aH9jqq3kRwOtwQoh2YhHDA) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for Minor spec add clearing in general content. Shapeable. Lethophobia (or "fear of forgetting", seeing as you're asking) offers +20 Mobility and +6.25% movement speed as the Lightweight Frame bonus but, honestly the stats are poor and it's difficult to recommend for PvE. If you do want to try it out, the tactic with Bowstrong and Arrow perks is to reduce the draw time and improve accuracy. As the support perk in the first trait column, Successful Warm up improves draw time further, although you don't have to get a kill first, Explosive Head in the second as the signature perk is the reliable boost to damage and flinch that it always is. The Dragon's Vengeance origin trait can be useful, although it only procs once your health bar turns red or an ally dies. You'll get a reload and 11s of a boost to range and either charge rate or handling. Recommended MW: Draw Time, with a damage boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Lethophobia DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): " Lethophobia is already making a strong impression on crucible players everywhere being the first bow to roll Disruption Break. Paired with any kinetic weapon you can city shot and get a whopping 50% extra damage on any kinetic weapon from you or anyone on your team. This damage debuff stays applied for a full 5.5 seconds. Be on the lookout for the “Disrupted” debuff off your screen, you will be extra vulnerable for this time." Recommended MW: Accuracy.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Likely Suspect DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Likely Suspect is like off-brand Snorri-FR5. It doesn’t have the room clearing perks like Snorri-FR5, nor does it have the boss killing power of Cartesian Coordinate. It is a fine neutral option while you farm out world drops to get Snorri-FR5.". Recommended MW - Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Likely Suspect DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "In terms of PvP Likely Suspect falls off in comparison to Cartesian Coordinate. The two most important stats on fusions as of this current sandbox is zoom, followed by stability. Ideally stability through perks so you can also bump up things like charge time and handling. Likely Suspect does not have the zoom to compete with Cartesian Coordinate, but does have consistency through perks. Bumping that up can give you a leg on Cartesian Coordinate but don’t expect those deep voops like Main Ingredient.". Recommended MW - Stability.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Likely Suspect DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's WQ] [MW: Stability / Charge] "Likely Suspect is fantastic. I have a really hard time recommending anything other than Firmly Planted / Successful Warm Up here, as it's a stellar workhorse, and just straight up deadly in PvP, and everywhere else. If you're crouch/slide averse -- Perpetual or Heating Up are strong options here too. There's a careful balancing act for PvP Charge Time I won't get into (look up Castle Content for a full breakdown), but for PvE I wanted pure consistency at a distance outside of Rapid Fire's normal operating range, so Smallbore + Particle Repeater were my go-to's." tags:pve

rolls.heading: Lingering Dread DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "In PvP you could try for chill clip but I think the utility of demolitionist for potentially shooting twice in quick succession is better. You’ll also need to have good aim to get those direct hits. Whereas with demolitionist if you do happen to get a kill with Lingering Dread you’ll just get some of your grenade cooldown knocked off. Proximity grenades are the best in slot for demoltionist, but if you wanted the alternative high-velocity rounds for getting the grenade to targets with chill clip is the way to go." Or it can be a generic scout rifle through Subsistence or Rapid Hit." Recommended MW: Reload Speed.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Live Fire DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Similarly to Live Fire’s PvE performance, its PvP performance doesn't stand out. It's not the most lethal scout to ever exist, nor is it the most consistent, nor does it have the most insane stats. If you want to use Rimestealer on a scout rifle then farm out a Live Fire. If you're okay without this one method of getting Frost Armor then you're okay with passing this weapon." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Loaded Question Perks and God Rolls
Locus Locutus DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Adamsdown_Boy - information as at v8.0.0.6 PvE / PvE-Boss / PvE-BossSpec / PvE-EndGame / PvE-God / M+KB / Controller): PvE pick for killing boss-tier Subjugators. As per @mossy_max (https://x.com/mossy_max/status/1804893174965338387) - boss-tier Subjugators have a 20% damage reduction to all non-matching damage, going up to 30% DR to all damage after they "screech", which counts as a shield in-game and reacts to shield-based trait perks. Locus Locutus - a pretty unloved Season of the Witch adaptive sniper is shapeable, so you can craft exactly the roll to help. First, Arrowhead Brake to help with recoil (if you just drop one, Chambered Compensator or Polygonal Rifling will also to help land consistent crits). Then Appended Mag which, with a Backup Mag mod, gets you to 7 in the mag (again, if your drop one, Extended Mag works too). Enhanced Overflow in the first trait perk column overflows the mag to 15. Finally, Under-Over provides a base +50% damage against the boss Subjugator "shield". The Head Rush origin trait mostly pointless - unless you make of habit of crouching and then standing up to shoot. Recommended MW: Stability, with a Backup Mag mod

rolls.heading: Locus Locutus DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "In PvP Thoughtless is hands down better. Thoughtless can roll Snapshot or Headstone. Perpetual Motion for a better handling bonus. The only reason you want to use Locus Locutus over Thoughtless is due to Opening Shot being stacked on Keep Away. This way you can stack fully into handling as enhanced Opening Shot and Keep Away with Accurized Rounds puts you at a flat 100 range. While not the most optimal in most Guardian's eyes, it will still be a solid weapon to use." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: Lodbrok-C DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast Versus Enemies episode 51 with @ImpetusAlways, @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5BpRdHN5pWyyKNLGsLB1Qh?si=4b9f4a74bf194ff2). First-choice PvE roll for end-game content. Pretty decent, in-bands stats, so Smallbore offers a little extra range and stability, both of which are useful boosts. To take full advantage of Target Lock, to burst down beefier minors, look for as large a magazine as you can and combine with Fourth Time's the Charm. This could be a little niche, but this roll with Lodbrok could be a useful tool to have in your loadout if you're facing constructs and vehicles, with the Hakke Breach Armaments' 15% damage boost on top of Target Lock. Recommended MW: Stability or Range, with a damage-boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, Controller, M+KB

rolls.heading: Lodbrok-C DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "Lodbrok-C in PvP will be an interesting weapon. High-impact frames have some chunky damage and allow for some great team shooting potential. Add in some juiced up stats and range and you can compete with some of the more meta weapons. You can realistically hit 100 range which will put you at a whopping 40m of in-game range. Juice up your other stats through perpetual motion, add in kill clip for extra oomph." Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Lonesome DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S16] [MW: Range] "I know a few people who main Lonesome. Sidearms have been slowly becoming more and more prevalent in PvP, likely due to the operating ranges being moved slightly out in-line with pellet Shotgun nerfs, Slug buffs, and SMGs being more widely used. Lonesome feels comfortable in a variety of ranges, with a pretty hefty Range stat, very nice TTK potential, and sporting Swashbuckler that allows for some pretty great punishes when getting rushed." tags:pvp

rolls.heading: Love and Death DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [MW: Velocity] "Love and Death is fantastically versatile, and currently the easiest HGL to farm for, full stop. Pre-S15, this would have been the best future-proof HGL for DPS (should they ever be buffed), with the ability to stack your mag with Clown Cartridge, auto-reload, and sporting tied-highest Velocity for 150RPMs. You will [always] want Spike Grenades, at a massive 50% damage increase, then Quick/Hard Launch depending on your Handling preference. Full Court is great in theory, but inconsistent due to the accuracy at range required, so super situational. Demolitionist is great for auto-reloading mid-DPS, and Chain Reaction makes it a fantastic workhorse for general use. (tags: god-pve)|tags:god-pve

rolls.heading: Lubrae’s Ruin Perks and God Rolls
Luna Regolith III DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 77 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/4RxkTOwLINWvKzUitNoAFy?si=1426d1f760c544b5) with @ImpetusAlways and @Saint_Kabr while @CourtProjects is on holiday. First-choice PvE pick for burst damage against Major Spec enemies in end-game content. Named after the unconsolidated debris that covers the moon's surface, the first thing to say is that, if you already have a decently rolled Aggressive Sniper Rifle (in the hope that they get a touch of love in a future balance pass), you can move along as there's nothing to see here. The stats are weak in a currently (as at v7.2.0.3) weak subtype, but if you're looking for a more easily obtained weapon, it's still very usable. It's a Vanguard weapon, so there is a huge trait pool - if you do want the best chance of a usable drop, reset your ranks a few times first, before you focus your engrams. As with all Sniper Rifles, Handling is king - there should be time between shots for the reticule to settle, so you don't need so much stability, making Fluted Barrel the top choice, with Arrowhead Brake, and Corkscrew Rifling as alternates. Tactical Mag gets you four in the mag, which is the same as Appended or Extended, so is the top choice there (although Extended Mag + Backup Mag does get you to 5 total). In the first trait column as your support perk, you can make an argument for Triple Tap or Clown Cartridge, both of which can get you to 5 shots without reload with a base mag of 4, or 7/8 shots if the base mag is 5. Alternatively, Field Prep speeds up reloads and boosts reserve ammo. In the second trait column, as your signature perk, Explosive Payload is the best option for additional damage for solo play, or Firing Line works in a fireteam. While it's not a game-changer, the origin trait's Wild Card submunitions can be fun to help damage nearby adds on a kill. Recommended MW: Handling or Reload Speed, with a damage-boosting (Major or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod (only with Extended Mag).|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-God, PvE-EndGame, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Luna Regolith III DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "In PvP there are really only a few options. Max handling with Snapshot Sights and Opening Shot will be the most sought after roll. But I wouldn't be opposed to Eye of the Storm, Moving Target, or even one of my favorite silly perk combos: Opening Shot and Under Pressure. Previously this was only on the Occluded Finality with its monstrous zoom. That also helped in bullet bending, but as the majority of players prefer short zooms this will allow other players to enjoy my silly combo." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: Malediction DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "I've heard some incredible things about Malediction. Mostly that it feels like a 140 but hits like a 120. Enlightened Action is the best perk for 120s, it bumps up two of the lower stats of the archetypes; handling and reload. Explosive payload also bumps up the damage ever so slightly. Harmony and Discord work really well together to constantly have a full magazine and a damage bump for 7 seconds. You won't be able to keep up the damage bump, but the synergy is there." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:mkb,controller,pve

rolls.heading: Marsilion-C DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Blast Radius. Rapid-Fire(150) Solar Grenade Launcher Heavy Ammo.  "Marsilion-C is an interesting grenade launcher. It doesn't do anything special to set itself apart from other grenade launchers in its class but it is craftable. Meaning you can create your 5/5. If you like pure damage you can roll with Field Prep for faster reloads and more reserves and your choice of damage perks. Full Court for longer ranged damage phases, Explosive Light stacked with surge mods, and Vorpal for plain Jane damage. Or you can invest more into add clear with Envious Assassin to overflow the magazine (also good for quicker damage phases) and Incandescent for ‘splosions!" |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Marsilion-C DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Blast Radius. Rapid-Fire(150) Solar Grenade Launcher Heavy Ammo.  "In the crucible you might want a different grenade launcher. Perpetual Motion is the best in slot for column three and Impulse Amplifier is the best for Column four. On other grenade launchers like Memory Interdict you can roll Impulse Amplifier with a better fourth column perk for either damage or consistency. Marsilion-C is great if you don’t have a grenade launcher for PvP but I would not rely on it being the best." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Nightshade DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 78 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5KOQQ9QtvSEhRVb4vjeO3B?si=8c4784e02b87424e) with @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects. First-choice PvE pick for add-clearing Minor Spec enemies in general content. Nightshade is back - the same lightweight frame as in vanilla D2, which now gives you +20 Mobility and +6.25% to movement speed, but this time as a Strand weapon. The stats are solid by comparison with other kinetic options. Start by straightening up the recoil direction with Arrowhead Brake, and then add one of the combat effect rounds - Ricochet also adds stability, but they are all useful. In the first trait column as the support park, either Enlightened Action boosts reload speed and handling to maximum in two 3-round bursts, or Outlaw maximises reload and applies a scalar. To pair with this in the second column signature slot, Collective Action boosts damage by 20% for 7s on an Elemental Pickup, or Kill Clip adds 25% damage for 5s after a precision kill. The Nadir Focus Origin Trait adding range and accuracy on repeated hits isn't game-changing, but it is nice to have. Recommended MW: Range, with a damage-boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Nightshade DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Nightshade in PvP has had an interesting run. From being a viable option in Vanilla, to being passed over with random rolls, to being an off meta choice in its revival, to being outclassed, and finally returning again. What a ride. Arrowhead Brake is again my choice in barrels, but if you can control the recoil direction you can certainly opt for other options. Ricochet Rounds is the best in slot whenever it is available so please choose that. High-Caliber Rounds can work but I find neutrally the bump in stability outweighs possibly flinching off my opponent. In the third column it is all about Keep Away. Being able to get a range bump for free is incredible. I prefer Kill Clip for an easier time 1v2'ing as I am usually in a bad situation, but Headseeker is better neutrally for any 1vX situation as it's always on. With my roll choices you can have 100 recoil direction with a counterbalance mod and 32m of in game range. Plenty to take advantage of the bonuses of Keep Away." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: No Survivors Perks and God Rolls
Nox Perennial V DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 78 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5KOQQ9QtvSEhRVb4vjeO3B?si=8c4784e02b87424e) with @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects. First-choice PvE pick for burst damage against Major enemies and sustained damage against Bosses in end-game content. In case you're wondering, the name translates as "enduring" or "recurring" night - Nox Perennial is the first high-impact fusion rifle and, although the stats aren't top notch, this roll is worth watching for, for Majors and Boss phases (for when you're running Tractor Cannon, say). As with all high-impacts, the handling is terrible - Arrowhead Brake adds a moderate amount as well as taking the recoil direction to 95, but Fluted Barrel and Corkscrew Rifling are decent alternatives. You could go in a couple of different directions with the battery perk - Ionic Battery adds two shots (don't worry about the reload speed penalty, we can deal with that later), or Accelerated Coils ups the first rate, but Liquid Coils is probably the top choice - again we'll use a trait to reduce the charge time from three business days to something more usable. In the first trait column as the support perk, Envious Assassin can boost the battery to 15 charges with 14 rapid kills with other weapons, while Controlled Burst adds 20% damage while reducing charge time for hitting all your bolts. The Wild Card origin trait is great for PvE - the occasional drop of Telesto-like "Experimental submunitions" on a kill for a bit of free additional damage. Recommended MW: Charge Time or Handling, with a damage-boosting (Major, Boss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Nox Perennial V DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "In PvP there’s a similar but not quite the same story. Under Pressure with Kickstart is going to be the main play, but Elemental Capacitor is also great for boosting some lower stats. I wouldn’t take this fusion into PvP unless you know you like High-Impacts but it could serve you well, again, in loadout flexibility." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: Occluded Finality Perks and God Rolls
Ogma PR6 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "But it also has some incredible utility outside of being a funny name. Adaptive munitions and disruption break together create an incredibly easy to use weapon that scales up to higher tier content with ease. The lower base damage of being a lightweight frame does hurt it a tad, but for the most part that hasn’t stopped people from using things like the BxR Battler in all content either. You could also opt for demolitionist and dragonfly for maximum uptime on those weakening grenades I see everyone loves so much. There’s a lot that can be done with this pulse, so don’t instantly scrap it." Recommended MW: Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Old Sterling DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Reload Speed. Adaptive Frame(600) Strand Auto Rifle Primary Ammo.  "It’s interesting to see the old Scathelocke model being used. I know many vets who loved this auto and I can’t blame them. Reliable was the name of the game back in Vanilla. In today’s world the best roll to look out for is similar to Rufus’s Fury. The benefit Old Sterling has is in its frame. Being an adaptive frame might feel better to some users compared to Rufus;s Fury being a rapid-fire frame. Entirely dependent on how you like to shoot. Frenzy and Demolitionist I listed as being better perks only because Hatchling falls off incredibly quick on non-Strand builds. The extra damage from the Threadlings are nice, but don’t pack as much of a punch without Strand to buff them." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Old Sterling DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Stability. Adaptive Frame(600) Strand Auto Rifle Primary Ammo.  "Adaptive autos have not been meta in quite a while. It was back when Hard Light was too strong that adaptive autos felt like they had a place. What a weird time to be playing Destiny. It was like a light show all the time in PvP. Nowadays you’re going to have to do a lot to make adaptive autos feel strong. Surplus will bring up all your stats. Harmony when paired with a special weapon can also make multikills feel like a breeze if you couple the two. A fast handling, good feeling auto with a damage bump. Takes a lot to make this auto work, but I’m sure someone wants to." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Opaque Hourglass Perks and God Rolls
Optative DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Precision frame hand cannons have not had their time in the spotlight for a while. Outside of the Seventh Seraph Officer’s Revolver paired with Lucky Pants there are not many reasons to use a precision frame hand cannon. If you run void subclasses a lot you can take advantage of Golden Tricorn to maximize damage, making these fast firing hand cannons feel way more chunky than they normally are. Rapid Hit will also bring up the already decent reload speed and their exceptional stability. If you feel like you need more range in PvE (which is an already odd thing to task for) you can opt for Keep Away for a continuous reload speed buff and a touch more range." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Optative DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Keep Away is stronger in the crucible where range matters far more. Kill Clip gives you nostalgia for the Not Forgotten or Luna’s Howl days of yore with a 0.67 time to kill. You are put in a time constraint which some players might not like, but in 6v6 game modes it’s not too hard to find another unwilling participant. Fully invested into range your Optative can reach out to 34.66 meters, for the record that is further by over 1 meter than catalyzed Thorn. The drawback is the lower 1.0 s TtK but once you get going with the 0.67 TtK you can destroy any Thorn user. (Food for thought)." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Ouster Engine Perks and God Rolls
Out of Bounds DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S18] [MW: Reload] "Out of Bounds is good. It's really, really good. Almost half the perks on this thing are good. Its stock recoil direction is good. Its stock handling is good. The Reload sucks, but then it comes with Threat Detector. It comes with Subsistence. It comes with Demolitionist/Golden Tricorn. Or Adrenaline Junkie. It's got Threat Detector/Kill Clip. Its one of the best perk pools I've seen. Potentially ever. So it really just comes down to your personal preference here - do you like the gun? Cool. Max out the reload and go ham. Have fun." tags:pve|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Out of Bounds DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 278 with @Critbuff. First-choice PvP pick for duelling. As will all playlist weapons, the more reputation resets you can get, the more trait options you'll get to play with, otherwise you've only got a 1/144 chance of any pair of traits dropping, before you even look at barrel, mag and masterwork, although at least 50% of them are good PvP traits, which makes getting a usable roll a little easier. You have access to an excellent PvP origin perk for SMGs in Omolon Fluid Dynamics, in a solid SMG subfamily, needing only the smallest of damage buffs to go from a TTK of 0.67s with 11 crits and 1 body, to a 0.6s 8 crit, 2 body kill. Stats are on the low side, and with a Zoom of only 14, you'll never compete with a range specced Multimach or Shayura's, so focus on making this as good a CQC SMG as you're likely to meet. Add as much Handling and Stability as you can with barrel and mag so you can keep the reticule on target for crits. For a duelling roll, it's not really necessary to look further than Dynamic Sway Reduction in the first trait column - pair this with the Omolon origin trait and Tap the Trigger and you'll end up with a precision bullet hose no matter where you are in the magazine. Recommended MW: Stability or Handling, with a Targeting Adjustor, Quick Access Sling or Sprint Grip mod depending on your preference.|tags: PvP, PvP-Duel, PvP-God, MKB, Controller

rolls.heading: Outlast DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Stability. Rapid-Fire Frame(540) Solar Pulse Rifle Primary Ammo.  "Outlast is a great gun with awful stats. Arrowhead Brake is almost mandatory for bringing up the low recoil direction, but you might also want to throw on a CB mod for 100 recoil direction. Flared Magwell would be good for the stat bump and reload speed. Feeding Frenzy is the best for not needing precision hits, but Outlaw is great in the event that you can handle the bounce. Kill Clip is an easy 25%, but you could also opt for solar grenade builds with Demolitionist, or Dragonfly for some better add clear." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Outlast DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Stability. Rapid-Fire Frame(540) Solar Pulse Rifle Primary Ammo.  "In PvP you will still want Arrowhead Brake. Ricochet Rounds is perfect for bringing up the low stability. Rangefinder currently is best in slot and you can couple that with Kill Clip for bonus damage. Competing slightly with Horror’s Least and slightly with Autumn Wind." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Palmyra-B DIM Voltron PVP recommendation

rolls.heading: Parabellum DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 934 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/0M24D3moUjRCXj0AtIsCCx?si=208c6d31bdb7453e) with @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for add-clearing Minor spec enemies in end-game content, particularly with a solar subclass. Parabellum has decent stats and a nice clean sight picture. Round out the range, stability and recoil direction stats to your preference with the barrel perk. You're looking for a snappy reload, so Alloy Mag is the best mag perk choice, but the stability from Tactical Mag and Flared Magwell make Parabellum easier to control. Heal Clip in the first trait column and Frenzy in the second work well - Frenzy boosts reload speed (and handling) to 100 anyway as well as adding a +15% damage boost. The fast reload, means it's easier to proc Cure from Heal Clip, which you can use with Ember of Benevolence on a solar subclass. The Field-Tested origin trait is a slow burn but, if you can stay alive, the stacks do build up to have a pretty significant impact on the gunfeel stats. Recommended MW: Reload Speed, with a damage boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Parabellum DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Adaptive frame submachine guns are my favorite in PvP. They combine a good weapon feel with good damage too. The Hero’s Burden is amazing with Kill Clip. Parabellum gets two damage perks both with different feels. Adagio will give you forgiveness and keep your time to kill relatively the same. If you can activate Frenzy you’ll drop your time to kill down to a blistering 0.67s but you’ll have to keep Frenzy up in order to maintain that momentum. I have a difficult time playing the mini game of “Frenzy.”" Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Pardon Our Dust DIM Voltron PVE recommendation

rolls.heading: Pardon Our Dust DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 279 with @Saint_Kabr. First-choice PvP pick for duelling. Shapeable. The Hot Swap origin trait works well for PvP, boosting handling when swapping to the weapon while damaged for quick swaps and ADS speed. For direct, one-hit kills, you'll need Spike Grenades, paired with a barrel that boosts velocity. In the first trait column, Auto-Loading Holster means you can carry on about your business and have a shot ready when you need it, or Steady Hands provide a boost to handling when you swap back to your primary. In the second trait column Demolitionist or Pugilist grant a hefty 20% ability energy on a kill. Recommended MW: Handling or Velocity, with a Quick Access Sling or Sprint Grip mod depending on your preference.|tags: PvP, PvP-Duel, PvP-God, MKB, Controller

rolls.heading: Path of Least Resistance DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Adamsdown_Boy - information as at v8.0.0.6 PvE / PvE-Boss / PvE-BossSpec / PvE-EndGame / PvE-God / M+KB / Controller): PvE pick for killing boss-tier Subjugators. As per @mossy_max (https://x.com/mossy_max/status/1804893174965338387) - boss-tier Subjugators have a 20% damage reduction to all non-matching damage, going up to 30% DR to all damage after they "screech", which counts as a shield in-game and reacts to shield-based trait perks. If you do have a Path of Least Resistance - still obtainable from the Seraph Shield mission - it's shapeable, so you can craft exactly the roll to help. First, a barrel that helps with stability, to keep your reticule steady will help land consistent crits. Then, as a Backup Mag mod gets you to the maximum of 107 shots, Light Mag or Tactical Mag boost reload speed. In the first trait perk column, Adaptive Munitions increases damage to non-matching Subjugator "shields" by for a total damage boost of around +45% after 15 hits (+73% when enhanced). In the second column you can stack damage further - with Focused Fury for +20% once you've hit crits with half the mag, or if there are minor adds around, One for All grants +35% damage for 10s when procced. You could use the Ambush origin trait for a little extra damage, or Omolon Fluid Dynamics for bonus stability at the start of the fight. Recommended MW: Stability, with a Backup Mag mod

rolls.heading: Path of Least Resistance DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "Trace rifles have had a very small but solid cult following. The best are still the exotics, but having a near-perfect hipfire juiced up auto rifle is hard to argue against. Path of Least Resistance doesn’t offer a lot in terms of lethality to keep the ball rolling once it does get started. Neutrally it is very strong but I think PvE is where it will survive." Recommended MW - Reload.|tags:pvp,mkb

Perfect Pitch DIM Voltron PVE recommendation

Subsistence and Incandescent are such a strong pairing together. Onslaught, while fun, doesn’t get as much magazine back with Subsistence so you’re bound to run out of ammo eventually. It will be great if you don’t have a CALUS Mini-Tool or another solar SMG." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Perfect Pitch DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "To the Pain is interesting but really forces you into using Perfect Pitch, while we have some good perks, nothing that really stands out. Onslaught is a great perk for 6v6 in kill chaining. Incandescent allows for Solar synergy and AoE damage but without a really strong kill chaining perk like Kill Clip and more range this weapon falls short, quite literally, of other SMGs in its class." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Perses-D DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Perses-D has only a few good things going its way. The first being the “for all” twins; stats for all and one for all. Bumping up the lower stats all 150s have and boosting damage with the same activation. Other combos are the tried and true rapid hit paired with vorpal or explosive for decent damage but lower ceiling." Recommended MW: Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Perses-D DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "High-impact scouts have a weird spot within the crucible sandbox. With Vostok and Eternity coming back it allows some more long ranged options to dominate PvP. But Perses lacks in the two departments I want; ideally it would be snappy and have good stability to make my side easier in long range. You can fully send into handling and stability to offset the lower base stats, but you can’t go half way with it. Steady rounds on a scout rifle isn’t the worst idea because of the weapon types inherent range, so the loss of range really won’t hurt it either. Perpetual motion and either explosive payload or opening shot are the ideal perks to go really incentivise good crucible habits and make your shooting experience better, but if you don’t feel like moving around a lot you can opt for rapid hit instead to stay firmly planted at B flag." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Persuader DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 78 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5KOQQ9QtvSEhRVb4vjeO3B?si=8c4784e02b87424e) with @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects. First-choice PvE pick for short phases of sustained damage against Boss Spec enemies in end-game content. Even with some decent stats, honestly, there are better rapid-fire sniper options out there for sustained boss damage in a short damage window (think Caretaker or Golgoroth), but if you haven't got one of them already, then Persuader can be made to work in the meantime. Start with Arrowhead Brake to control the recoil and boost handling, and add Tactical Mag for 7 shots in the chamber (Appended and Extended Mag offer nothing better). The combination of Rapid Hit in the first trait column as the support perk - which adds stability, maximises reload speed together with a reload animation scalar - and Triple Tap in the second column as the signature perk, which lets you get off 10 crit shots without reloading, should provide a decent stopgap. The Nadir Focus Origin Trait adding range and accuracy on repeated hits isn't game-changing, but it is nice to have. Recommended MW: Range, with a damage-boosting (Boss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvEBoss, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Persuader DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Persuader also falls behind in the Crucible. It does have access to Surplus to reach 100 handling but you would either have to have all 3 stacks or 2 stacks with full investment into handling to maintain ability usage while keeping 100 handling. If you can maintain all your Surplus stacks and invest the rest of your stats into range you'll have 87 range and 100 handling and reload speed. Still incredible but then you remember it's a rapid-fire frame meaning you can't crit supers or double body." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: Phoneutria Fera Perks and God Rolls
Phyllotactic Spiral DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Reload. High-Impact(340) Arc Pulse Rifle Primary Ammo.  "Phyllotactic Spiral almost needs Arrowhead Brake to be viable in PvE. Using your mod slot on a counterbalance mod means you’re giving up on damage through a backup mag or spec mod. Compulsive Reloader is the only good reloader perk which can pair wonderfully with Voltshot or Kill Clip. Frenzy is a great neutral option that can or can’t make use of Compulsive Reloader." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Phyllotactic Spiral DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Stability. High-Impact(340) Arc Pulse Rifle Primary Ammo.  "In PvP you can run a counterbalance mod or Arrowhead Brake if you prefer a different mod. Keep Away is the in slot and paired with Headseeker you will have a great neutral pulse rifle. If you get the jump on someone you’ll have the bonus range and a near perfect first burst. They turn on you, start flinching you, headseeker then comes into play to keep you on that two-burst. Kill Clip is another viable option for a more forgiving two-burst after your first kill, especially when paired with Tunnel Vision. Elemental Capacitor will only accentuate the neutral game of this pulse no matter what subclass you use. Whatever you use this pulse will be a great addition to any vault." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Piece of Mind DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "What would make Piece of Mind better than say Time-Worn Spire or a Gridskipper alternative? Well, it can get barrels to boost it above Time-Worn Spire, and its perk pool is better for neutral builds in PvE. [...] It won’t have the straight up consistency as Time-Worn Spire but it will have a higher ceiling for using it in all activities." Recommended MW: Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Piece of Mind DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "Time-Worn Spire has better range and consistency perks, while you really need arrowhead break to clean up the recoil direction to a usable degree. It does allow for other consistency in the form of perpetual motion and moving target or elemental capacitor; it falls just a little short compared to the zoom of Time-Worn Spire. Both are equally good options." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Piece of Mind DIM Voltron recommendation
Pizzicato-22 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: PvE first-choice roll for general content. Good overall stats, but has 1 less point of Zoom than its competitors, so you might need to add a little extra range to make up for that with the barrel and mag choices - Extended Barrel does this and take the Recoil Direction to a maximum 100. Ricochet Rounds then pushes Range out by a total of about 1.25m, and smooths things with a dab of Stability too. To make the most of Multikill Clip, you need to spend as little time reloading as possible - Perpetual Motion (anywhere) or Ensemble (in a fireteam) both help with this. Recommended MW: Range or Reload Speed, with a damage-boosting (Minor, Major or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-God, Controller, MKB

rolls.heading: Pizzicato-22 DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP Controller): Recommended MW: Range “The good pizza cutter does not stop at PvE, no it carves right through PvP like a hot pizza cutter through pizza (am I making you hungry yet?) Rangefinder and eye of the storm make it immediately competitive, but I would recommend bumping up the recoil direction to 100 through your choice of sauce, chambered compensator or extended barrel (input depending). Top it off with ricochet rounds, garnish it with a range masterwork. Cook it on high with your favorite shader. Boom bam, pizza cutter-22. you are welcome“ (Year 5 S18: Season of Plunder Solar Submachine Gun Primary Ammo)|tags:PvP, Controller

rolls.heading: Planck's Stride DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S18] [MW: Reload] "Planck's Stride is a unique LMG. Typically, I aim for strong stability, and ways to force a reload to make LMGs viable, as no one wants to spend 5 full seconds reloading mid-combat. Planck's doesn't really have that outside of Grave Robber, which means using an LMG for CQC engagements to get the most out of its perk pool. I can see it working with ranged melees, like with Warlock's Celestial Fire - a synergistic Karnstein, Grave Robber / Pugilist build could be fun. Ultimately, there's a few interesting options here for build-crafters, but not a lot to help it win out against better LMGs." tags:pve|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Planck's Stride DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP Controller): Recommended MW: Stability “PvP does not fare much better for the new machine gun. You can bump up the range, stability, and recoil direction and it still kicks like a drunken mule. That also takes away from eye of the storm, damage perks like rampage (not on this gun) or swashbuckler. 900 rpm machine guns need a lot of work just to feel good. Once they feel good, you remember 450s exist and move on to those.“ (Year 5 S18: Season of Plunder Arc Machine Gun Heavy Ammo)|tags:PvP, Controller

rolls.heading: Positive Outlook DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Stability. Precision Frame(450) Void Auto Rifle Primary Ammo.  "Positive Outlook was one of my favorite guns from Year 1. I still have mine in my vault with 4,600 kills on it. Nothing better than old reliable Kill Clip from Curse of Osiris. This new version gives us access to Golden Tricorn, Destabilizing Rounds, and even Repulsor Brace making it a really great void option for anyone drawn to the void. Ambitious Assassin isn’t a replacement for reload, but it does help make reloads feel a bit further away instead of making them faster." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Positive Outlook DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Stability. Precision Frame(450) Void Auto Rifle Primary Ammo.  "In PvP I cannot reliably see a way to make Positive Outlook meta. Tap the Trigger and Kill Clip will give you the best footing for multikills and sprees. Eye of the Storm also helps win duels for a more consistent 1v1 experience. Both Zen Moment and Dynamic Sway Reduction could swap in for Tap the Trigger as your consistency perk." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Pressurized Precision DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Charge Time. Adaptive Frame(660) Strand Fusion Rifle Special Ammo.  "Pressurized Precision is a great choice for a Strand fusion rifle. Discord will regenerate ammo for you as long as you get the kill, which will pair well with High-Impact Reserves or Vorpal for damage. Hatchling does not refund you ammo, but does pair well with Strand subclasses. Use whatever build you’d like." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Pressurized Precision DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Range. Adaptive Frame(660) Strand Fusion Rifle Special Ammo.  "In PvP I don’t think Pressurized Precision is the best in its class. You can also get away with the Discord shenanigans but with zoom decoupling from Rangefinder soon I wouldn’t rush out to get one. Firmly Planted did receive a nerf a while back and the effects can still be felt, it is not as consistent as it used to be. You can still make it work, but I would look elsewhere for a better fusion." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Prodigal Return DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Reload. Lightweight(90) Arc Grenade Launcher Special Ammo.  "Prodigal Return is an interesting pairing with Envious Assassin to overflow the magazine and with Disruption Break means you will always have at least 1 or more shots to break the enemies shield at all times. It’s like a better Auto-Loading Holster if you have a great add-clearing weapon to pair with it. This will always be your go to." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Prodigal Return DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Velocity. Lightweight(90) Arc Grenade Launcher Special Ammo.  "In PvP not actually having Auto-Loading Holster or Slideshot is a bummer. Psi Hermetic V DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 78 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5KOQQ9QtvSEhRVb4vjeO3B?si=8c4784e02b87424e) with @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects. First-choice PvE pick for Stasis synergy and add clearing Minor Spec enemies in end-game content. The name's a bit confusing - Psi (Ψ) is the 23rd letter in the Greek Alphabet, and Hermetic can mean something that's occult or mysterious and difficult to understand, relating to the legendary alchemist Hermes Trismegistus, tightly sealed, immune to external influence, or solitary like a hermit, so your guess is as good as anyone's. Anyway, this is a Stasis High Impact pulse rifle with decent stats. Look for something that helps with recoil direction, handling, stability or a mix as the barrel perk, and add range with Accurized Rounds or the useful gunfeel boosts of Tactical Mag. The traits are a bit PvP focussed - Enlightened Action is the best of the first column support perks, for big boosts to handling and reload speed with just two 3-bullet bursts. For Stasis synergy, Headstone generates a crystal on a crit kill, to use to proc your subclass fragments. The Wild Card origin trait is great for PvE - the occasional drop of Telesto-like "Experimental submunitions" on a kill for a bit of free additional damage. Recommended MW: Charge Time or Handling, with a damage-boosting (Major, Boss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Psi Hermetic V DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 78 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5KOQQ9QtvSEhRVb4vjeO3B?si=8c4784e02b87424e) with @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects. First-choice PvE pick for add clearing Minor Spec enemies in end-game content. The name's a bit confusing - Psi (Ψ) is the 23rd letter in the Greek Alphabet, and Hermetic can mean something that's occult or mysterious and difficult to understand, relating to the legendary alchemist Hermes Trismegistus, tightly sealed, immune to external influence, or solitary like a hermit, so your guess is as good as anyone's. Anyway, this is a Stasis High Impact pulse rifle with decent stats. Boost range with your barrel and magazine perks - Full Bore or Hammer-Forged Rifling and Accurized Rounds adds up to 3.62m of ADS range. In the first trait column as the support perk, Heating up will straighten up the recoil direction and add stability, while Frenzy in the second column as the signature perk adds 15% damage and maximises handling and reload speed. The Wild Card origin trait is great for PvE - the occasional drop of Telesto-like "Experimental submunitions" on a kill for a bit of free additional damage. Recommended MW: Charge Time or Handling, with a damage-boosting (Major, Boss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Psi Hermetic V DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "So Psi Heretic V is a great PvP pulse. Don’t believe me? You can hit 100 stability with Smallbore / Steady Rounds / Void Elemental Capacitor / Stability MW, and you have your fourth column perk to do with as you please. Perpetual Motion for more handling and reload, Headseeker for more forgiving bursts, Kill Clip for a bit of chaining, Headstone for Stasis shenanigans. The world is your oyster. Crack it and find out what you want." Recommended MW: Stability. tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: Punching Out DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S16] [MW: Range] "Punching Out ships with some lethal combos, such as Overflow/Subsistence and Surrounded, Threat Detector, Frenzy, Swashbuckler... Nailing down a decent roll of this isn't easy, with a whopping 12 perks in each column. Are Sidearms that viable in endgame? They've had some niche usage in CQC Lost Sectors with Unstoppable Sidearm - they've got potential if we see some real close-quarters encounters with tougher enemies. Until then, its worth keeping one in your back pocket." tags:pve

rolls.heading: Punching Out DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by Destiny Massive Breakdowns from Podcast 235. PvP first choice roll. Suros Rapid Fire sidearm subtype is a bit of a sleeper as as Season 15, or you want to stray off-meta a bit. Recommended MW: Stabilty or Reload Speed.

rolls.heading: Pure Poetry DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 278 with @Critbuff. First-choice PvP pick for duelling. As with all playlist weapons, the more reputation resets you can get, the more trait options you'll get to play with, otherwise you've only got a 1/144 chance of any pair of traits dropping, before you even look at barrel, mag and masterwork. Out of the box TTK  of 1s with 2 crits and 1 body - the subfamily's low stability makes a third crit more difficult. Stats are average, so this won't replace a well-rolled Palindrome or Eyasluna. If you are duelling, then round out the stats with Fluted Barrel and Ricochet Rounds or Flared Magwell. Then, for duelling, Well-Rounded or Perpetual Motion fill out the stats nicely, and Eye of the Storm makes hitting your third shot in a gunfight easier. Recommended MW: Handling, with a Targeting Adjustor, Quick Access Sling or Sprint Grip mod depending on your preference.|tags: PvP, PvP-Duel, PvP-God, MKB, Controller

rolls.heading: Pure Poetry DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB PvE Podcast 32. PvE first-choice roll for end-game content. Drops from Vanguard activities, so reset your reputation as many times as you have patience for first, to maximise the trait options from decrypting engrams. Maybe not meta as at S18 v6.2.0.6, but Aggressive Frame HCs are always handy for bounties or patrols that call for crit kills, anyway. Good stats overall, slow firing, so stability is less important - add Handling with Fluted Barrel and add a few extra bullets with Appended Mag. For your end-game needs, Ensemble or Perpetual Motion and Frenzy in combination will passively boost gunfeel stats and add 15% damage. Recommended MW: Stability or Handling, with a damage-boosting (Minor, Major, or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-God, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, Controller, MKB

rolls.heading: Qua Furor V DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies episode 136 from 1 April 2025 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/6I828LxsIqdth4iIlSYRmG?si=8022df8ede664223) with @CourtProjects, @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-God PvE-EndGame M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE pick for clearing Minor adds, Elites, Champions and Minibosses in both general at-level or better, and end-game underleveled content. Qua Furor V is in a good subfamily for damage so if you don't have a decent stasis LMG, it might be worth a look. At base, by comparison with its peers, the stats are poor - 34 Range equates to 46.81m ADS and 29.26m hipfire before damage dropoff, 48 Stability is ×1.1630 flinch resist at 100 Resilience, 42 Handling is 0.39s ready, 0.41s ADS and 0.37s stow speed, 40 Reload Speed is a 5.04s reload(!), Recoil Direction is 58, Zoom is 16, and magazine size is 63 (max 91). With 0/1/2/3 reserves mods, total ammo is 435/489/544/571 (max 571). You'll probably need a bit of help with either Recoil Direction or Stability to help hit crits. Then, looking at the glacial reload time, Reload Speed is the priority but the combat effect magazine perks are also decent. In the first trait perk column, if you're clearing adds, it should be easy to get to five stacks of Feeding Frenzy for maxxed out Reload Speed and a ×0.8 animation multiplier. For damage, One For All provides ×1.35 damage for 10s for hitting three enemies or Dragonfly provides a Stasis AoE explosion on a crit kill. If you're interested, Qua Furor translates from Latin to "as rage" or "in the capacity of rage". The Wild Card origin trait is fun, dropping Telesto-style "experimental submunitions" on kills for additional add-clearing potential. Recommended MW: Reload Speed or Handling, with a Backup Mag mod or, when masterworked, Synergy to create Stasis shards or, when the artifact mod's around, Stunloader. Also, don't forget to check whether there's a useful Seasonal Fragile mod for the activity you're running.|tags:PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-God PvE-EndGame M+KB Controller

Qua Xaphan V DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 77 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/4RxkTOwLINWvKzUitNoAFy?si=1426d1f760c544b5) with @ImpetusAlways and @Saint_Kabr while @CourtProjects is on holiday. First-choice PvE pick for sustained damage against Boss Spec enemies in end-game content. The name literally means "in the character or role of Xaphan" - a fallen angel that rebelled with Satan, and a demon of the second order associated with fire, which doesn't really fit with a void weapon, but there you go. To be honest, if you have a decently rolled void boss-damage LMG, you can ignore Qua Xaphan - it has pretty bad stats. If you are looking for a more easily obtainable one to fill a gap, remember this is a Gambit weapon, so reset your reputation a few times before focusing any engrams. Handling is the usual play for Aggressive LMGs, so Fluted Barrel is the top barrel choice, with Arrowhead Brake and Corkscrew Rifling as decent alternates. Any of the combat effect mag perks (Ricochet Rounds, High-Caliber Rounds or Armor-Piercing Rounds) will work, or Flared Magwell is an option to speed up reload speed. As the first trait column support perk, particularly for sustained boss damage, Triple Tap and Clown Cartridge work - Triple Tap can add up to 12 additional shots, and Clown Cartridge between 5 and 22. For additional damage, in the second column support perk, you can hit the higher damage stacks of Target Lock with your overflowed rounds, take a straight 15% damage (and maxed handling and reload speed) with Frenzy, or 20% additional damage in a fireteam with Firing Line. Recommended MW: Handling or Reload Speed, with a damage-boosting (Boss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-God, PvE-EndGame, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Qua Xaphan V DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "There are not many uses for a high-impact machine gun in PvP. They require all crits but just feel extremely unforgiving. Anything to bump up the consistency will be greatly appreciated. Dynamic Sway Reduction or Under Pressure, and Ricochet Rounds will feel great. Rampage might not be the best but it does allow you to 3 crit 1 body any resilience and 2 crit 2 body all the way up to 7 resilience. Adrenaline Junkie isn't quite as strong on the low end. But if you can get a grenade kill you can 3 crit any resilience for a 0.33s TtK. Incredibly fast." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: Quickfang DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S16] [MW: Impact] "Quickfang can hang with the big dogs if you nail down the jump>heavy>light attack combo, and has been known to (slightly) out-perform Guillotine (we're talking about 3%) with that method. Assassin's Blade sets your movement speed to max sprint, which allows chaining kills to become easier, and One-for-All is nice for encounters like Zulmak in Pit of Heresy, or Phalanx Echo, where you can cash in on a 35% damage buff fairly easily." tags:pve

rolls.heading: Qullim’s Terminus God Roll and Perk Pool
Ragnhild-D DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "For the non-crucible members, our boy Reginald will feel good as either a major clearer or champ destroyer. One-two punch and frenzy are both incredible options that don’t need a lot to feel strong. Demolitionist can help with those grenades.
