rolls.heading: Ragnhild-D DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "This puts Reginald directly in competition with its energy alternative A Sudden Death. Both with elemental capacitor and quick charge hit 100 handling. Which can further be boosted by steady hands or perpetual motion. Which means you can completely forego handling in favor of more range for deeper clean ups. Range does not increase your one hit kill range, but will make it more consistent in those further ranges when needing slight damage. I’d argue investing into range would be a better pairing than assault mag as the RPM bump isn’t enough to even catch up to precision frames." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Ragnhild-D DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Player Versus Enemies Podcast 11. PvE first choice roll for end-game content. Shapeable weapon. It's difficult arguing against a Handling boost for your barrel, and then Assault Mag or Tactical Mag are the best magazine options. Use Auto-Loading Holster to be certain when you pull it out, it's ready to roll, or Perpetual Motion to help with boosts to all the useful stats, with the reliability of One-Two Punch for close-up major and champion damage. Recommended MW: Reload Speed or Handling, with Backup Mag or damage-boosting (Major, Boss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Randy's Throwing Knife DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Stability. Rapid-Fire Frame(260) Kinetic Scout Rifle Primary Ammo.  "Randy’s Throwing Knife is back! My favorite little scout-auto love child. This time with random rolls!!! I do enjoy seeing older ritual / pinnacle weapons refreshed with more options, especially post sunsetting. It does feel good grinding them out again. Randy’s Throwing Knife was the best rapid-fire scout for its time but unfortunately fell into a time where scouts were just awful to use and did not have a set place outside of MIDA Multi-Tool or a few specific options. Randy’s Throwing Knife with Kinetic Tremors will feel great as it fires fast enough to get the AoE back to back. Cascade Point is really funny to me, seeing a 260 scout bumping the RPM all the way up to 428 via D2Foundry. If this lands for me I don’t even really care about viability, it will just be fun." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Randy's Throwing Knife DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Stability. Rapid-Fire Frame(260) Kinetic Scout Rifle Primary Ammo.  "Randy’s Throwing Knife did find its slight niche within PvP, hopefully the new version delivers. I think it does and then some. The old version had Rapid Hit and Kill Clip, which you can get again, but with Extended Mag instead of a better mag boosting perk. You also have the option of bumping up the recoil direction making those quick headshots a lot easier. While the old Randy’s Throwing Knife didn’t have a bad roll, getting it again with slightly better options feels good." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Rapid Growth Perks and God Rolls
Razor's Edge DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "A contender to our beloved spin-to-win, Falling Guillotine. Now if you already have a good Guillotine or Hero of Ages you really don’t need Razor’s Edge, but having the option is never a bad thing. It also looks incredible as a mini-light saber.". If Razor’s Edge also got eager edge you can really be a menace being removed from radar and flying around. But being a standard vortex frame hinders its ability to perform in all aspects of PvP.". rolls.heading: Reckless Endangerment DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / PvP / M+KB / Controller): "In PvE there are just better shotguns, where a neutral, high-handling weapon just will not fit the bill. In Reckless Endangerment’s benefit is how smooth it feels to use. No other shotgun really comes close to how fluid this feels without any investment. [...] Crucible is where Reckless Endangerment can really flex [...] Having a shotgun that you don’t really have to invest in to make it feel fluid gives new players and people uncomfortable with PvP a shotgun to compete with. The obvious comparison is Riiswalker and I do think Riiswalker takes the cake over Reckless Endangerment in all forms of PvP. That being said, you can use Reckless Endangerment and hang with Riiswalker without feeling like a detriment to your team or yourself.". Recommended MW - Handling.|tags:pve,pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Recurrent Impact DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Having a huge magazine and access to firing line gives some wonderful damage but you really have to crank straight down on your input method of choice to hit constant crits. Without arrowhead brake you best hope you see yellow because a recoil direction of 53 is pitifully low. You could also opt for a room clearing weapon through subsistence and headstone, but I would just rather have Eyasluna for that. Even Syncopation-55 is better than this bullet hose." Recommended MW: Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Recurrent Impact DIM Voltron PVP recommendation

rolls.heading: Redback-5si DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Sidearms in PvE are in a weird spot, while they have high damage per shot and excel in close range, you don’t always want to put yourself into those situations. The best perk for the Redback-5si is surrounded with surrounded spec, but that is a hairy situation to begin with. I would bump up its mag, range, and spec into reloader perks to maximize this sidearm’s usage, but I just don’t think it beats submachine guns, let alone autos or other longer ranged primaries." Recommended MW: Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Redback-5si DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "While I love this archetype for PvP, I just cannot bring myself to like Redback-5si. Veist weapons feel good, I enjoy the archetype. But something about this sidearm just doesn’t feel right. It could be the lack of power behind its shots, hit registration being wonky, or just the sound not sitting well with me. But ultimately Redback-5si is an alright sidearm. Not having high-lethality perks is a downer, but I can get past that with better neutral perks. You want to push out its range without sacrificing other stats to try and edge yourself into submachine gun territory to find the best success." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

Reghusk’s Pledge Perks and God Rolls
Resounding DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies episode 133 from 11 March 2025 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/49VFDRj05iDXTJDOBqQpga?si=e61731a19fb64938) with @CourtProjects, @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE pick for use against Minor add groups in both general at-level or better, and end-game underlevelled content. Resounding is only the second version of the Aggressive Frame fusion rifle subfamily, and the first in the kinetic slot, firing three horizontal bursts of four bolts in a gradually widening spread. At base, the stats are in-band by comparison with its peers - 35 Range and 15 Zoom equate to 21.35m ADS and 16.43m hipfire, 57 Stability is ×1.1416 flinch resist at 100 Resilience, 28 Handling is 0.46s ready, 0.36s ADS and 0.36s stow speed, 32 Reload Speed is a 2.26s reload and Recoil Direction is 59. Base battery size is 6 (max 7) and with 0/1/2/3 reserves mods, total ammo is 20/23/26/28 (max 28). Barrel and magazine perks are fixed - Smallbore and Particle Repeater providing a smidge more Range and Stability. The most useful trait combination is Subsistence, with kills refilling the magazine, to maintain Reservoir Burst's ×1.25 damage boost and AoE explosion on a kill when the battery is full (or nrealy full when enhanced). Of the three origin traits, One Quiet Moment is probably the most useful for PvE, boosting Handling by +20 and Reload Speed by +40 with a ×0.95 animation duration after 5s of not dealing or receiving damage, or and 1.5s after you get a kill. Recommended MW: Reload Speed with a Quick Access Sling mod (Backup Mag tanks your reserves, so avoid) or, when fully enhanced, Synergy to create Strand Tangles or, when the artifact mod's around, Stunloader. Also, don't forget to check whether there's a useful Seasonal Fragile mod for the activity you're running.|tags:PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-God M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Resounding DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "There's nothing too good for PvP in either column, which is super weird for Bungie’s ritual philosophy. Cornered puts you in a bad position and Hatchling only does significant damage with a fragment." Recommended MW: Reload Speed.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Retold Tale DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 91 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/50NPk7xF3Tt6X3DVucE9jU?si=44d2b7d44a5a4aec) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for add-clear of Minor Spec enemies in end-game content, particularly with a void subclass. Retold Tale has okay stats compared to other precision frame shotguns and the trait pool is weighted towards PvP, but there's still some nice combinations here. First, for the barrel perk, Barrel Shroud, Smallbore or Corkscrew Rifling all add useful stats and for the mag slot while Assault Mag or Tactical Mag are the top two choices, Light Mag or Appended Mag aren't bad alternatives. For your trait combination, this is the first pellet shotgun that offers the the trait combination of Repulsor Brace and Destabilizing Rounds for aggressive CQC add clearing. You don't need a void subclass to make use of them - they'll work perfectly well without - but you can improve the effect with void fragments. The Advanced Reflexes origin trait is quite useful in PvE - when activated by an enemy hit, it adds +30 Handling (or +20 charge rate with swords), for additional ready and ADS speed, together with a handy +20 Mobility, for 7.5s. Recommended MW: Range or Stability, with a damage boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) mod. Tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Retold Tale DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Retold Tale is not a PvE shotgun. It does come with some nice PvE perks like One-Two Punch, but the lack of Grave Robber lowers it in my opinion. Destabilizing Rounds and Repulsor Brace have good synergy but on a shotgun I’d rather take a good damage perk over an add clearing one." Recommended MW: Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Retold Tale DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Retold Tale was and still is one of my favorite shotguns. I used it a ton before getting my best Mindbender’s Ambition. I still prefer the U shaped sights on Retold. I grinded out a decent Compass Rose with QuickDraw and Snapshot Sights, so we will see if Retold will be better. I think most players will still opt for a Matador but having a super snappy shotgun won’t hurt your vault space too much (though seeing the state of your vault it might be a tough squeeze /s)." Recommended MW: Handling or Range.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Retraced Path DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Adamsdown_Boy - information as at v8.0.0.6 PvE / PvE-Boss / PvE-BossSpec / PvE-EndGame / PvE-God / M+KB / Controller): PvE pick for killing boss-tier Subjugators. As per @mossy_max (https://x.com/mossy_max/status/1804893174965338387) - boss-tier Subjugators have a 20% damage reduction to all non-matching damage, going up to 30% DR to all damage after they "screech", which counts as a shield in-game and reacts to shield-based trait perks. If you do have a Retraced Path from Dares of Eternity, it's shapeable, so you can craft exactly the roll to help. First, a barrel that helps with stability, to keep your reticule steady will help land consistent crits. Then, as a Backup Mag mod gets you to the maximum of 107 shots, Light Mag or Tactical Mag boost reload speed. In the first trait column, Adaptive Munitions increases damage to non-matching Subjugator "shields" by for a total damage boost of around +45% after 15 hits (+73% when enhanced). In the second column you can stack damage further - Frenzy is most straightforward for +15% damage (and maxxed reload speed and handling), slightly trickier is Focused Fury for +20% once you've hit crits with half the mag. If you're really good in a firefight and there are minor adds around, One for All grants +35% damage for 10s when procced and if you want to roll the dice, you might just be able to pull off the second stack of Golden Tricorn for +50% damage for 10s. You won't get too much use out of the Hot Swap perk except possibly for the initial swap to the weapon. Recommended MW: Stability, with a Backup Mag mod

rolls.heading: Retraced Path DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 279 with @Saint_Kabr. First-choice PvP pick for kill-chaining, particularly with a solar subclass. Shapeable. The Hot Swap origin trait works well for PvP, boosting handling when swapping to the weapon while damaged for quick swaps and ADS speed. Great stats - standard for trace rifles, but not quite as good as Hollow Denial. Difficult to work out what role it plays in the sandbox, effectively being a very accurate high ROF auto rifle that uses special ammo. The base accuracy-adjusted TTK is 0.8s wth 8 crits and 5 body shots, but small damage buffs do make it easier to use - Golden Tricorn x1 or Radiant get you to 0.73s TTK in 5 crits and 7 bodies, and if you can get Golden Tricorn x2 up (and have enough battery charge left), you can get an Accuracy-Adjusted TTK of 0.53s with 5 crits and 4 bodies. Choose a barrel that doesn't have any penalties and Tactical Battery provides a range of small gunfeel stat boosts. In the first trait column, Well-Rounded or Perpetual Motion both help further with gunfeel stats and, in the second, Golden Tricorn is the best damage boost for PvP. This could be very good with a Hunter Caliban's Hand build... Recommended MW: Stability or Handling, with a Targeting Adjuster, Quick Access Sling or Sprint Grip mod depending on your preference.|tags: PvP, PvP-Duel, PvP-God, MKB, Controller

rolls.heading: Retrofit Escapade DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "I dislike 900 rpm machine guns. They don’t feel very strong for how fast they fire. If I had some good Rambo moments where I could shred hordes of low-tier adds without having to recoil control that much I would love it. But having the lowest base stats across the machine gun archetypes and the fastest rpm makes them unwieldy. Arrowhead brake will bring up the terribly low recoil direction and having more in the magazine means you can be off target for 0.001s and not waste half your ammo. Feeding frenzy and field prep are both ways to bump up the decent reload speed. Golden tricorn will get you the best bang for your glimmer when paired with a void subclass. All void subclasses are viable in end game content so that’s not an issue. Frenzy is best when not on a void subclass and vorpal will allow you to do increased but not substantial damage in and out of combat." Recommended MW - Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Retrofit Escapade DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "Let me issue you a challenge. If you kill me in PvP with this weapon I will uninstall Destiny 2. [...] In other news, go for consistency perks as the base stats are so low they are almost non-existent. Arrowhead break will make the recoil easier. Accurized rounds will push out your effective range. Heating up will better your stability and recoil direction further, but you have to get a kill first. Tap the trigger won’t last long, but will help you get some damage on target." Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Retrofit Escapade DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB PvE Podcast 44. First-choice PvE roll for general content. Shapeable. The Ambush origin trait is probably more for PvP than PvE, with the extra range, handling and 10% boost to damage for a few moments after combat starts, although Suros Synergy's flinch reduction in reload is useful in a firefight too so take your pick. This is better in every stat than the only other rapid fire LMG - Shattered Cypher - and there are a few ways you can go with a weapon build. You'll need to control your fire with short bursts - so, to make it easier, tighten up the recoil direction with Arrowhead Brake. For damage against a single target, you want your magazine as large as it will go, so look for Extended or Appended. Then extend the magazine with Fourth Time's the Charm, or improve your ammo reserves and reload with Field Prep in the first trait column to give Target Lock's 40& bonus to damage as much uptime as possible. Recommended  MW: Stability or Reload Speed, with a Backup Mag or damage-boosting (Major, Boss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-God, PvE-EndGame, Controller, M+KB

rolls.heading: Retrofuturist DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 93 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/0uYGSQqlyX1faCyATCDuno?si=IMSCRgTRQr-_THJdjxFMIQ) with @ImpetusAlways and @Saint_Kabr. First-choice PvE pick for burst damage against Major spec enemies in end-game content. Unfortunately, Retrofuturist is distinctly meh, so it's only worth hanging onto a roll if it fills a hole in your vault, and even then you'll have to reset your Crucible rank a few times to stand a decent chance of the roll you're after. As a Lightweight shotgun, you do get the +20 mobility and +6.25% movement speed and, for some reason, it has a base mags size of 7, but that's where the praise ends. If you're hanging onto one, try a barrel perk that adds Handling, although a bit of range and stability doesn't hurt either. Assault Mag and Tactical Mag are the best mag perk choices, but Appended Mag or Light Mag aren't bad. For burst damage against majors, the trait combination of Auto-Loading Holster and Trench Barrel is decent enough. Recommended MW: Handling, with a damage boosting (Major or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Retrofuturist DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller / M+KB): "If you like snappy shotguns you will be happy to learn that Retrofuturist gets access to Quickdraw and Snapshot Sights. I prefer Slickdraw as shotgun aim assist doesn’t feel incredibly strong to me, but I also don’t mind lower AA weapons in general. Having a fast shotgun is more important to me, your mileage may vary with that. I think most players will opt for the tried and true Quickdraw and Snapshot Sights." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,controller,mkb

rolls.heading: Royal Executioner DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Datto (PvE, MKB): "Something that looks quite appealing is Envious Assassin and Reservoir Burst. You overflow the magazine, you're getting bonus burst shots, what's not to love about that? And Envious makes a bit more sense on this gun vs. a primary weapon because the primary weapon is the thing you'll be using the most, at least in theory anyway. Or you're using a machine gun, but the concept is the same. The only other thing I'd want is maybe trying out Incandescent instead of Reservoir Burst for some scorch plays, killing two enemies at a time can spread scorch quite quickly. If you wanna do some PvP, Firmly Planted is the way to go in the first column. Arrowhead Brake, Accelerated Coils or Enhanced Battery and masterwork... reload speed probably."|tags:mkb,pve

rolls.heading: Salvager’s Salvo Perks and God Rolls
Scalar Potential DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 92 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/2qpoxXfXzBXohHBrdQnHbd?si=aH9jqq3kRwOtwQoh2YhHDA) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for bulkier Minor spec add clearing in general content. Shapeable. Scalar Potential has decent stats and a nice clean scope, but you're limited by a PvP trait perk pool. Arrowhead Brake is probably the best barrel perk, although you could imrpve handling or range if you can handle the recoil direction. Tactical Mag or Flared Magwell are then the pick of the mag perks. As the support perk in the first trait column, the new-for-season-23 Permeability works well with Golden Tricorn in the second as the signature perk to make the second damage stack, for +50% damage, attainable for any subclass you run. The Dragon's Vengeance origin trait can be useful, although it only procs once your health bar turns red or an ally dies. You'll get a reload and 11s of a boost to range and either charge rate or handling. Recommended MW: Range, with a damage boosting (Boiss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Scalar Potential DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "I hope that Scalar Potential lives up to its name. An arc rapid-fire frame Feels. Very. Familiar. Huh, I can’t place my thumb on it. Well, since we’ve never seen one of these before you’re going to want Overflow to double your magazine capacity, and Golden Tricorn to really pump up that damage. Arrowhead Brake is almost required for the low 53 recoil direction. With our new deterministic recoil you might be able to learn the recoil pattern and not need Arrowhead Brake, but I don’t like to fight with my weapons." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Scalar Potential DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Scalar Potential doesn’t reinvent the wheel. The perks unique to it aren’t useful in the crucible spare Enlightened Action which will make this pulse feel better but not help its lethality. Neutrally with Headseeker you could have a pulse that feels fantastic, but Keep Away will probably be better for the range and accuracy bonuses. If you like the model and sights I’d suggest grinding out the patterns, if you already have another rapid-fire frame pulse I wouldn’t worry too much about this one." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Scathelocke DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S15] (PvE): [MW: Stability / Range] "Scathelocke returns from its sunsetting slumber with a clear goal in mind - fill the near-empty space where 600RPM Kinetic ARs should have been for the last 280 days. Now one of three in the slot, its direct competition are The Last Breath and The Forward Path (previously the only 600RPM in the slot). Both Scathelocke and The Last Breath suffer from "Year 1 Syndrome", being weapons that kick like a mule. Arrowhead Brake transforms the gun, giving it a better 90 recoil direction, and its new perk combinations rock -- you'll want Subsistence/Rampage to mow down waves of adds, something that was noticeably lacking from The Forward Path."|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Semiotician DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "While this rocket is better than Crowning Duologue, it's because the bar was so low Semiotician almost tripped walking in this season. Semiotician has way too many vowels to be considered healthy, but has a decent enough perk tool to keep it around. Enhanced Field Prep and Enhanced Explosive Light are going to be tried and true. Thanks to one of my Patrons, we discovered with one reserve mod and enhanced Bipod you can have 15 rockets in reserves. The last possible perk combination is Impulse Amplifier with Frenzy as once Frenzy gets going you won't need the reload, so the added velocity is appreciated here." Recommended MW: Velocity.|tags:pve,controller,mkb

rolls.heading: Semiotician DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "High-Impact Frames are generally regarded as the best rocket archetype to use. Great blast radius for splash damage. Semiotician doesn't have any incredible AoE perks like Chain Reaction or Cluster Bomb for multikills but it does have Explosive Light to max out the blast radius, it's easy enough to get orbs, and you'll be able to nuke supers with Explosive Light." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: Senuna SI6 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Reload. Omolon Adaptive(491) Stasis Sidearm Primary Ammo.  "Boy do I love these Omolon sidearms. They just feel so good to use. Omolon Fluid Dynamics aside, these have always been one of my favorite classes of sidearms. Being that Senuna SI6 is stasis, the best perk for it is Headstone. Being able to slow and deal AoE damage off of a single kill is extremely strong. We all know how strong Disorienting Grenades are. Slow is just a different version of that. I don’t have to tell you all that. Feeding Frenzy is your best bet if you are “maining” this sidearm, but Envious Assassin could be fun. I don’t love it on primary ammo weapons as you should be using these the most, but depending on your build you could be swapping more than me. Another great perk to look out for is Golden Tricorn. Less useful if you don’t use Stasis, but extremely strong now with our current surge mods." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Senuna SI6 DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Range. Omolon Adaptive(491) Stasis Sidearm Primary Ammo.  "Senuna SI6 is arguably the best three-burst sidearm. Slickdraw gives you 100 handling. A counterbalance mod gives you 99 recoil direction. You can then invest entirely into range as Omolon Fluid Dynamics will give you your stability. The only issue is the fourth column perk. Headstone is great in 3v3. Elemental Capacitor can bump up your stability on void, or forgo a counterbalance mod on Stasis. If you jump a lot then Strand could be a great option for you. Headseeker is another good option. Ideally I would run Headstone just for locking down that position and having some cover as well. Certainly a gun I will farm for and then throw in my vault to use on random loadout shenanigans." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Seraphine Haze Perks and God Rolls
Servant Leader DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 275 conversation with @DefyFly. First choice PvP roll for kill-chaining. Drops from Gambit, so reset your reputation as many times as you have patience for first, to maximise the trait options from decrypting engrams. You don't need extra range,  and the high base zoom (26) means you can control the recoil with Stability, and not worry about Recoil Direction, in your barrel and mag. Kill Clip, in the second trait column, reduces the TTK to a competitive 0.7s (3 crits, 1 body), as at Season 18 v6.2.0.6, and you can pair this with Tunnel Vision or Rapid Hit, both of which will work well. Recommended MW: Handling or Stability, with a Radar Tuner (to help you stay aware of your surroundings when hardscoping), Quick Access Sling or Sprint Grip mod depending on your preference.|tags: PvP, PvP-Chaining, PvP-God, MKB, Controller

rolls.heading: Sleepless DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 91 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/50NPk7xF3Tt6X3DVucE9jU?si=44d2b7d44a5a4aec) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for add clearing Minor spec enemies in general content. Like other High-Impact Rocket Launchers with their high blast radius, Sleepless really only has one role, and that's add clearing - maybe for Gunsmith bounties. If you're going that way, you could maximise the Blast Radius with Volatile Launch as the barrel perk, and then boost Velocity with High-Velocity Rounds or reload Speed with Alloy Casing in the mag. You might as well have as many rockets to shoots as you can, so Field Prep boosts your reserves by one and reload speed further, as the first trait perk to support Chain Reaction in the second to really make sure that everything in the blast radius is dead. The Advanced Reflexes origin trait is quite useful in PvE - when activated by an enemy hit, it adds +30 Handling for additional ready and ADS speed, together with a handy +20 Mobility, for 7.5s. Recommended MW: Velocity or Reload Speed, with a damage boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) mod. Tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Sleepless DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Crucible rockets need one thing, a great blast radius. Sleepless has that in droves. With a blast radius masterwork you’ll sit at 100 even, that way you can invest your other perks into velocity and handling, bringing up some of its lower stats. With Quick Launch and High-Velocity Rounds you’re looking at a nice 60 velocity. It won’t beat Impulse Amplifier for the quickest rocket but it will move. Chain Reaction then becomes great for dealing with multiple guardians in every gamemode." Recommended MW: Velocity.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Sola's Scar DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S16] [MW: Impact] "Sola's is one of two Legendary Caster Frames, and the only one of the two that matters. Whereas Temptation's Hook (S11) has 46 Impact (considerably less than every other Sword), seemingly intrinsic to being a Caster Frame - Sola's has the regular 60, making it objectively stronger. It makes you wonder whether this was intentional to give the Trials Sword significance, or they forgot that Caster Frames are supposed to be weaker. Either way, Chain Reaction's fun, Frenzy's strong, Vorpal is a good backup." tags:pve

rolls.heading: Solemn Lie Perks and God Rolls
Solemn Remembrance Perks and God Rolls
Something New Perks and God Rolls
Something Something DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @Saint_Kabr, @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-Boss PvE-SustainedDamage PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE roll for sustained boss DPS in both general at-level or better, and end-game underleveled content. Something Something is an aggressive sniper rifle, which are rated C-Tier for endgame PvE by @TheAegisRelic tiering spreadsheet https://linktr.ee/TheAegisRelic offering high damage per shot but still usually outclassed by rapid-fire frames. The frame intrinsic offers no bonuses as per the https://www.destiny2.science/ Destiny Data Compendium. The stats are solid by comparison with its peers - hopefully when the website's back https://d2foundry.gg/w/3421075982 will show how the weapon stats impact in-game performance. First priority with the barrel perk is Handling, and then with the magazine perk look for something that helps improve magazine size - ideally you're aiming for 5 shots with this roll. For end-game use, Triple Tap in the first trait perk column generates one ammo from thin air for hitting three crits within 2s (3s when enhanced) of each other - so a magazine of 5 means you can fire 2 additional free shots without reloading if you land your crits. In the second column, for damage with a Prismatic subclass, Elemental Honing grants an escalating stack of damage up to ×1.4 for 20s (23s when enhanced) for each elemental damage type inflicted, which can be refreshed with a new damage type until you've run through all 5. There's also Kinetic Tremors emits three 6m AoE Kinetic shockwaves after 3 hits (2 when enhanced) within 3s of each other, which are treated as weapon damage so inherit damage boosts. The Vanguard Vindication origin trait provides up to ×3 stacks that reduces incoming damage at max to ×0.94 for a refreshable 10s (12.5s when enhanced), on kills within 20s of using a class ability. If you're interested, it's thought the name is from "Something, Something, Something, Dark Side", the Family Guy parody of The Empire Strikes Back. Recommended MW: Handling, with a Backup Mag mod.|tags:PvE PvE-Boss PvE-SustainedDamage PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Something Something DIM Voltron PVE recommendation

Stay Frosty DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies episode 126 from 17 December 2024 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/6SVRlV4ttvPkAm9HA44Km0?si=a9d7a25536b747c3) with @ImpetusAlways, @CourtProjects and @Saint_Kabr (PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE pick for Minor adds in general, and end-game underleveled content, particularly on Stasis builds. Stay Frosty has lost some solid PvE trait perks in its reprise so there's not as much to grind for in 2024-25. At base the stats are a little sub-par - 38 Range equates to 32.05m when ADS and 18.85m hipfire, 67 Handling is a 0.33s ready, 0.33s ADS and 0.31s stow time, and 56Reload Speed is a 1.78s reload. The Lightweight frame provides +20 to mobility and ×1.0625 movement speed. On M+KB you should push out range a bit with the barrel perk and then you can touch on reload or push the range out further with the magazine perk. The trait perk combination of Rimestealer and Headstone is a natural pairing, particularly when synergising with Stasis fragments, generating crystals to use and shatter, to grant a stack of Frost Armor (2 stacks when enhanced). The Dawning Surprise origin trait is pretty potent for PvE - 6 Minor add rapid kills drops a "gift" for your fireteam which on pickup grants +20% energy to all your abilities, restores 22HP and starts health regeneration. Recommended MW: Range, with a Backup Mag mod or, when masterworked, Synergy to create power orbs or, when the artifact mod's around, Stunloader.|tags:PvE PvE-MinorSpec PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Stormchaser DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Adamsdown_Boy - information as at v8.0.0.6 PvE / PvE-Boss / PvE-BossSpec / PvE-EndGame / PvE-God / M+KB / Controller): PvE pick for killing boss-tier Subjugators. As per @mossy_max (https://x.com/mossy_max/status/1804893174965338387) - boss-tier Subjugators have a 20% damage reduction to all non-matching damage, going up to 30% DR to all damage after they "screech", which counts as a shield in-game and reacts to shield-based trait perks. Stormchaser is the adaptive burst LFR that's still obtainable from the Duality dungeon. First, Arrowhead Brake to help with recoil, or Chambered Compensator or Polygonal Rifling will also to help land consistent crits. Ionized Battery with Backup Mag max out the battery at 7 shots, or Backup Mag alone provides 6, in which case op for Accelerated Coils for better burst damage or Particle Repeater for more stability. In the first trait column, Clown Cartridge can get you to 11 shots, or Rapid Hit can make landing crits easier and boost reload speed. In the second, Adaptive Munitions provides a +30% damage boost after 3 hits. The Bitterspite origin trait is useful, boosting reload if you're taking damage. Recommended MW: Charge Time, with a Backup Mag mod

rolls.heading: Stormchaser DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "I think Stormchaser is a weird weapon for the crucible. It does do enough to body shot with the whole burst, but you’re better off going for a crit with just a regular linear fusion rifle or body shot with adagio. Stormchaser is cool and I think it will see some use, but I don’t think it will be anyone’s first pick." Recommended MW - Charge Time.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Strident Whistle DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S18] [MW: Draw/Reload] "Fantastic Bow. Uses the Wolftone Draw-style model, with a stellar perk pool. Includes fan favourite Incandescent, Explosive Head, Vorpal for Champions, the list goes on. Excellently replaces the sub-par Solar Bow from Last Wish." tags:pve|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Strident Whistle DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Precision frames are some of the better choices for bows in PvP. Strident Whistle would need to have the lowest charge time of 576 to even compete in PvP. Fiberglass arrow shaft brings the accuracy back up after elastic string lowers it. Archer’s tempo is still great here, as is explosive head for double flinch. Moving target is a great pick if you feel you use your bow for follow up shots instead of bow-swap. Successful warm-up paired with archer’s tempo is incredible for some really fast draw times, but it feels almost “kill clip-esque” with successful warm-up needing a kill to activate." Recommended MW - Accuracy or Draw Time.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Subjunctive DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Subjunctive was the smg that could. It felt great in PvE and held its own back when recluse dominated all aspects of our life. Oh how naive we were. When Rampage and Outlaw were all we cared about. Things have shifted and more weapons are certainly popular. The only true competition Subjunctive needs to worry about is the Ikelos SMG. Both are arc, but what gives Subjunctive the edge? Golden Tricorn. It’s not the highest uptime on Arc but you can chain back to back arc ability kills with some loadouts, this will give Subjunctive a 1.5x damage bonus over the Ikelos (this is without giving the Ikelos a damage perk of its own). Most players spec’ed into Voltshot which will clear rooms easier but have slight difficulties taking down beefier targets. With Golden Tricorn 2x you’ll have enough damage to look at enemies and they’ll become unalive from sheer admiration, and you’ll be able to chunk down majors with ease, Subsistence just keeps the chains rolling." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Subjunctive DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Subjunctive in PvP is a bit more subjective. Being a lightweight frame without any large range extending perks puts Subjunctive lower on the list of best SMGs within Shadx’s playhouse. Threat detector helps boost your stats within range, and Swashbuckler can help your next engagement when you ultimately melee your opponent dead." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,controller

Summum Bonum Perks and God Rolls
Supercluster DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 92 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/2qpoxXfXzBXohHBrdQnHbd?si=aH9jqq3kRwOtwQoh2YhHDA) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for fallback damage against Boss spec in end-game content, particularly with a Strand subclass. Shapeable. Supercluster is the first Strand pinpoint slug shotgun, and the good news is it has very competitive stats. The bad news is that they're not as great as they were a few years ago for boss DPS. Use the barrel perk to boost range and the mag perk to improve its utility - your preference between Assault Mag or Tactical Mag, or Appended Mag. Slice, as the first trait column perk, when procced after using your class ability reduces outgoing damage from a target by 40%, whether or not they're immune - very useful in boss fights or as part of a Bait and Switch weapon rotation. For a damage boosting perk in the second trait slot, choose between Surrounded, if you're likely to be in CQC with extra adds, or Vorpal Weapon for a reliable 15% boost to damage against Majors and Bosses. The Dragon's Vengeance origin trait can be useful, although it only procs once your health bar turns red or an ally dies. You'll get a reload and 11s of a boost to range and either charge rate or handling. Recommended MW: Range, with a damage boosting (Boiss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Supercluster DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Slug shotguns are not nearly as dominant as they used to be. They used to be a high skill, high reward weapon that prioritized good aim for in return a deep OHK. Threat Detector and Fragile Focus both boost your already good range and handling. That’s really all you’re going to need. Invest the rest of your stats into whatever you feel like you’ll need more. Handling outside of Threat Detector range or range for when your shields are broken." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Survivor's Epitaph DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S16] [MW: Range] "There's a fair few playstyles you can get out of Survivor's Epitaph. Aim for max Range where you can, and either buff yourself like crazy with combinations of Rapid Hit/Heating Up/Tunnel Vision, pairing with (Multi)/Kill Clip, or ground yourself with strong passive choices - Slideshot for extra range, aiming for flinch pressure, Rapid Hit/Eye of the Storm or Moving Target... There's a long list of ways you can get the gun to work for you, if you can manage to slim down its absolutely massive perk pool." tags:pvp

Sweet Sorrow DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "It’s almost a better Chroma Rush for majors while still having good utility in general content. This allows you to expand your loadouts and flex into kinetic (or stasis) special weapons while still having that good bullet hose in your back pocket. You seriously need to clean up the recoil direction so I think arrowhead brake is a must-have on any Sweet Sorrow you do end up keeping. [...] Triple tap and vorpal weapon together can help you shred some majors or champions, but the overall lower base damage leans me to recommending other weapons specifically for major killing. You can make it work but it’s not ideal." Recommended MW: Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Sweet Sorrow DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "This is a fun situation where PvE and PvP mix. Arrowhead brake is still the best barrel, and I would recommend a counterbalance mod to get that recoil to 100 and give you the ease of use to invest into range or more consistency perks. The “big” damage perks on Sweet Sorrow don’t lend themselves to the PvP landscape, but one could argue demolitionist is a lethality perk, especially with current Void 3.0 grenades. Truthfully I’d try and make this weapon as consistent as physically possible and spec fully into stability. Autos are not in the best place, especially with their requirement of time-on-target for optimal TtK." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Synchronic Roulette DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Reload. Precision(600) Strand Submachine Gun Primary Ammo.  "Similar to Round Robin I don’t super love Envious Assassin on primaries. It can be nice in swap builds or with another good add-clearing weapon. Kill Clip is interesting as if your magazine is overflown you cannot reload for Kill Clip’s bonus. I would take Envious Assassin as a nice bonus but not a feature you rely on. Target Lock is nice but a quick 25 percent damage bonus immediately is nice." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Synchronic Roulette DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Range. Precision(600) Strand Submachine Gun Primary Ammo.  "Slickdraw is an incredibly strong perk and this is the first submachine gun with it. You can invest entirely on range without having to worry about handling or using Peacekeepers. Target Lock is the perk of the season so definitely go out and throw this on against Immortals (Adepts). Kill Clip is another good bet if you want a more reliable damage bonus." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Syncopation-53 DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Syncopation is in an interesting spot right now. It competes with Eyasluna, the previous best headstone weapon while also being able to make crystals, but from further ranges. Being a pulse rifle puts it in a better spot for range and having multiple bullets per burst rivals hand cannon’s big single shot damage. The unfortunate part is no rapid hit to maximize the weapons feel, but it does have some incredible utility with stasis builds and general crystal shattering." Recommended MW - Reload.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Syncopation-53 DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "If one pulse screamed consistency this pulse would do it. PvP falls into one of two categories. Consistency or lethality. Syncopation doesn’t have anything to push it into the 2 burst realm outside of person buffs (empowering rift, inertia override, etc) but it does have utter consistency. Arrowhead cleans up the recoil perfectly, while high-caliber rounds makes it harder to get hit in return. Then the choice between moving target, the newly buffed hip-fire grip in combination with headseeker or rangefinder." Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Syncopation-53 DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Player Versus Enemies Podcast 13. PvE first-choice roll for stasis builds. Shapeable. Solid feeling weapon - could do with a tiny bit of help with recoil direction in the barrel, and then your choice of mag perk for stability, range, reload speed or combat effect. Maybe more PvP options with the traits, but Outlaw and Headstone provide a solid choice of weapon for stasis builds. Recommended MW: Handling or Reload Speed, with Backup Mag or damage-boosting (Minor, Major, or Taken Spec) mod.|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Taipan-4fr DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Speedrunner's Shopping List: Ranged sustained damage (good for: all S21 raids and dungeons)|tags:god-pve

rolls.heading: Taipan-4fr DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP Controller): Recommended MW: Charge Time or Range “Taipan-4fr is also great in PvP, fragile focus and opening shot stack to basically give you headshots. I would recommend lowering the charge time to make it shoot quick, and increase the handling to make it feel quick. Keep an eye out for this or craft it and get those perks at level 1.“ (Year 5 S18: Season of Plunder Void Linear Fusion Rifle Heavy Ammo)|tags:PvP, Controller

rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S16] [MW: Charge Time/Range] "Tarantula is the weaker of the Linear Fusion Rifle family, though it can still do a couple interesting things for picking off individual targets. Field Prep/Firmly Planted help with consistency, Pulse Monitor in a "dueling" scenario, Moving Target for the Aim Assist bump, and Box Breathing for a one-time damage boost per-shot." tags:pve

rolls.heading: Tarnation DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Tarnation is underwhelming. [...] Similarly, adaptive frame grenade launcher’s higher base blast radius lends them to being the better pick in PvP as well.". Recommended MW - Blast Radius.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Tarnished Mettle DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S18] [MW: Stability] "Tarnished Mettle has a number of great options for a Scout. Shoot to Loot/Explo is a tried-and-true combination for endgame, with Rapid Hit also being well-loved (for good reason). It's one of the privileged few to have Demolitionist in the third column, meaning you have access to Demo/Swashbuckler (for synergy with the Right Hook origin trait), Demo/Voltshot if you want the long-range jolting capability, and also Dragonfly which I'll never not recommend based on fun factor alone. While I don't see this being anyone's favourite gun, it does add some flavour to endgame Scouts." tags:pve|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Tarnished Mettle DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP Controller): Recommended MW: Stability “Crucible is not a nice home to 200 rpm scouts. In the MIDA archetype they fire fast, but are not super special. You can roll explosive payload for better outgoing flinch, but if that made them meta you would have already seen a bunch of Night Watches in PvP. These scouts even with the best of rolls is not anything to write home about.“ (Year 5 S18: Season of Plunder Arc Scout Rifle Primary Ammo)|tags:PvP, Controller

rolls.heading: Tears of Contrition DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S18] [MW: Handling] "Tears of Contrition is a Scout Rifle. It fires bullets. It fires them far. It has Explosive Payload and Vorpal. It looks cool. It's a Scout Rifle." tags:pve|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Tears of Contrition DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "Precision frame scouts do not have a strong presence in PvP. To give you the best chance using one, use arrowhead brake. That will clean up the recoil enough. There aren’t many lanes that require 80m+ of range, so going for steady rounds will make each shot easier to land. No distractions also combats a lot of the flinch scouts naturally take. Explosive payload increases your outgoing flinch. All of these steps should give you the best chance in the Crucible." Recommended MW - Handling.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Techeun Force DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Charge Time. Adaptive Frame(660) Arc Fusion Rifle Special Ammo.  "Techeun Force is looking to be the best fusion rifle in the game. I don’t say that often but given all the goodies of double damage perks, craftability, and great stats you can really have it your way. Rewind Rounds coupled with Collective Action for subclass agnostic buffs, Controlled Burst for laying down enemies or Golden Tricorn when on Arc will feel incredibly strong. Reconstruction is a tried and true gem of a perk, only beaten by my obsession with Overflow. Kill Clip can stack with all of these creating over 100% damage increases with Kill Clip, Golden Tricorn 2x, 3 Surge Mods, and Major Spec. If you set things up right you can almost have a 150% damage increase. Simply put it is the best fusion in the game for damage and has greater utility beyond that." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Techeun Force DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Range. Adaptive Frame(660) Arc Fusion Rifle Special Ammo.  "In PvP you could also recommend Techeun Force as the best fusion in the game and I will. Arrowhead Brake with a CB mod brings the recoil to 100, so you can invest the rest of the fusion into range via Projection Fuse and Rangefinder. If you value kill chaining you could go for Reconstruction and Controlled Burst for never reloading and getting that 20% damage increase. Another niche use case would be Kill Clip and Kickstart for a nice 4 bolt kill, you’d have to be sprinting immediately after reloading but the enhanced version extending Kill Clip’s timer will help you out." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Temptation's Hook DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "The last returning legendary this season is also one of the first for many. A caster frame in Destiny 2. You won't really be getting a lot of close hits with this so Tireless Blade is the best for at-a-distance fighting. But if you are rough and tumble close, then Relentless Strikes becomes the easy choice. Attrition Orbs is nice if you chuck your heavy in the general direction of enemies to occasionally poop out and orb. Collective Action doesn't work with everything but when it gives you a nice boost you really feel it." Recommended MW: Impact.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Temptation's Hook DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Caster frame swords feel like they should belong in the crucible. But it always feels like it's missing something. Either the animation is too long, the tracking is too slow, maybe the damage just isn't tuned right. Valiant Charge will help closing the distance if you use this and heavy attack a second person after lunging. Tireless Blade will give you some ammo back, but there's just something off." Recommended MW: Impact.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

The Comedian DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Player Versus Enemies Podcast 12. PvE first-choice for general use. Bad stats generally, and sub-optimal damage output as at Season 16 - rapid fire subtypes are generally better picks. If you want to tuck one away in case Aggressive frame shotguns get some love, or there's an anti-Champion mod around, you'll generally want good handling and faster firing speed or an extra round in the mag from your barrel and mag, and something that helps with ammo economy and additional damage from your trait columns. Recommended MW: Handling or Reload Speed, with Backup Mag or damage-boosting (Major, Boss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags:pve

rolls.heading: The Domino DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Future War Cult will always be the best cult, don’t even @ me. New Monarchy? Not a cult. Dead Orbit? Also not a cult. It’s literally in the name. Jokes aside, the Domino has some interesting but not meta shaking perks. Overflow and Subsistence will probably be what everyone gravitates towards while Shoot to Loot will be a more niche option. Incandescent is the only good perk in the fourth column with Harmony offering some niche but good damage alternatives." Recommended MW: Reload Speed.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: The Domino DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "The Domino has better perks for PvP but even then the best perk in the third column is Slideways, not even Slideshot or Snapshot Sights, Slideways. Opening Shot and Moving Target are both great for making sure you can hit headshots and Incandescent allows for some nice AoE damage but I think outside of people investing into this sniper it will not see much use." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: The Enigma DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S18] [MW: Handling] "Glaives are not what you think they are. Glaives are not melee weapons. They are instant-use, renewable (portable) shields, with a low skill floor and high skill ceiling. They're built around mid-health single-target cleanup, ranged instant kills, re-directing enemy aggro, and at worst, mitigating the consequences of poor gameplay decisions. Build into maximum Handling, with Impulse Amplifier being non-negotiable." tags:god-pve|tags:god-pve

rolls.heading: The Enigma DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 266 conversation with @legoleflash. PvP first-choice roll for duelling. Get the range up to 80-85 with Haft and Mag, then Impulse Amplifier improves your bolt speed further, and adds a little to the slow reload speed. Unstoppable Force adds 30% if you take damage to your shield, which can help finish off opponents with Restoration effects active. Masterwork: Range, with a Freehand Grip mod - this pairs well with a Glaive Targeting mod on your helmet.|tags: pvp, pvp-god, pvp-duel, controller, mkb

rolls.heading: The Epicurean DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S18] [MW: Charge/Stab/Handling] "There's something specific Epicurean does which few others do. Quickdraw/Backup Plan is an incredible combo - something akin to a hot-swap that lets you fire a burst off as quickly as a shotgun can. Similarly, Surplus is a nice option that gives more Handling uptime than Quickdraw." tags:pve|tags:pve

rolls.heading: The Epicurean DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "Rangefinder is the absolute best in slot perk for the Epicurean. Zoom is the most important thing on fusion rifles. While Main Ingredient still boasts the best in class zoom, the Epicurean can come pretty close. Snapshot sights or surplus will make the Epicurean feel lighter or handle better which is one of its lowest stats. Look for arrowhead brake to clean up the recoil and make those longer ranged voops easier to handle." Recommended MW - Stability.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: The Eremite DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "The Eremite is an interesting fusion. If you didn't farm out an Envious Assassin / Controlled Burst Loaded Question from last season you can get a solar version this season. You can even craft it to your liking. If you prefer Golden Tricorn for a higher damage ceiling or Reservoir Burst for clearing out rooms of enemies. The possibilities are truly yours to harness. Lead from Gold and Heal Clip can be really nice additions too depending on your full loadout." Recommended MW: Charge Time.|tags:mkb,controller,pve

rolls.heading: The Eremite DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "I'm sure most people have seen u/Legoleflash's new video on the Eremite (if you haven't, go watch it https://youtu.be/csuxec3lFcs). Essentially the main point is enhanced Slickdraw doesn't feel terrible and allows you to full send into range. With Slickdraw maxing out your handling you can even opt for handling reducing perks like Full Bore, or, better yet, Extended Barrel for a bump in recoil direction. If you don't like Slickdraw there you could opt for Offhand Strike and find a good balance of range and stability." Recommended MW: Range or Charge Time.|tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: The Last Breath DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB PvE Podcast 25. PvE first-choice roll for end-game content. Adaptive auto-rifles are still pretty competitive as at S17 v4.1.5.2. In the same slot as Duty Bound or Scathelocke, which both have better Zoom, so you might need a touch of either range or stability, depending on preference, to compensate. Arrowhead Brake helps tidy up the wonky recoil and one of the Combat Effect magazines adds a little range and some utility effect. For end-game, particularly solo, content, it's difficult to top Demolitionist and Osmosis to give you an additional element to help break shields. Drops from the second (Hexahedron Room) encounter if you're looking to farm it. Recommended MW: Stability, Range or Reload Speed, with a damage boosting (Minor, Major or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: pve, pve-god, pve-endgame, pve-solo, pve-minor, controller, mkb

rolls.heading: The Last Breath DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by Destiny Massive Breakdowns Podcast 233. PvP first choice roll, needs a fair bit of help to compete with Scathelock in kinetic slot, or Gnawing Hunder as an option. Recommended MW: Stability with Targeting Adjuter or Freehand Grip mod.

rolls.heading: The Long Walk DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB PvE Podcast 25. PvE first-choice roll for end-game content. Rapid Fire Snipers are, as at S17 v4.1.5.2, the best DPS option, but aggressive frames still put out decent damage. Keep shots on target with a stability barrel, then Appended Mag gets you to the max mag size, Clown Cartridge and Firing Line are the best way to get damage out as fast as possible in a fireteam. Drops from the first (Phalanx Echo) encounter if you're looking to farm it. Recommended MW: Stability or Handling, with a damage-boosting (Major, Boss or Taken spec) mod.|tags: pve, pve-god, pve-major, pve-endgame, controller, mkb

rolls.heading: The Long Walk DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by Destiny Massive Breakdowns Podcast 233. PvP first choice roll - can crit supers. Recommended MW: Handling

rolls.heading: The Long Walk DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S15] (PvE): [MW: Handling / Stability] "This is a super-versatile perk pool, so you're bound to find something you like. As a burst damage option, if they ever buff Firing Line, Clown/Overflow + Firing Line is going to make this incredibly strong. For PvP, as a more-farmable Frozen Orbit (72RPM), Snapshot/Killing Wind are great, and Arrowhead Brake lets you get 100 vertical recoil. On Console, I'd recommend investing in Stability if you're planning on firing multiple times, if not, Handling is quite important, as it leans more towards the sluggish Succession-level of Handling."|tags:pve,pvp

rolls.heading: The Other Half DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 101 from 17 April 2024 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/1CJQuqM89I7hWA9KYvjCop?si=arncPmZ1SseRp6Atuf1ilw) with @ImpetusAlways and @CourtProjects. First-choice PvE pick for Major and Boss Spec enemies in end-game content. The Other Half returns with a few new perks, so if you haven't got one yet Dares of Eternity awaits! Boost the damage with the blade perk, and then Swordmaster's Guard adds a little bit of everything useful. While Eager Edge was the unique Dares perk back in the day, it's appeared elsewhere since so Relentless Strikes is probably the more sensible first column trait perk to combine with Surrounded in the second for 35% (41.8% when enhanced) bonus damage when in a crowd. The Hot Swap origin trait is always useful for boosting charge time and guarding stats when you first swap to it. Recommended MW: Impact, with a damage boosting (Major, Boss or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-MajorSpec, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: The Other Half DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 280 with @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvP pick for kill-chaining. Shapeable. Swords aren't necessarily a top choice for PvP, as at v6.2.5, but this does come with a very useful perk in Eager Edge to close the gap unexpectedly on an opponent. Jagged Edge adds damage, and Swordmaster's Guard gets your power attack as quickly as possible. Eager Edge is your must have in the first trait column and Vorpal Weapon is the best of a very PvE orientated set of traits in the second. Recommended MW: Impact, with a Quick Access Sling mod.|tags: PvP, PvP-KillChain, PvP-God, MKB, Controller

rolls.heading: The Prophet DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 101 from 17 April 2024 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/1CJQuqM89I7hWA9KYvjCop?si=arncPmZ1SseRp6Atuf1ilw) with @ImpetusAlways and @CourtProjects. First-choice PvE pick for Minor Spec add clearing in end-game content. If you find a roll you like, keep for the ability to enhance perks when Final Shape arrives. The Prophet is the first stasis aggressive frame scout rifle and while it has good stats it's not a very competitive subtype for PvE unfortunately. It's got a bit of a kick so, even on M+KB you might benefit from boosted stability with the barrel and mag perks. The trait perks are a bit underwhelming too - Rapid Hit is probably best in the first column, with Demolitionist and possibly Keep Away also useful for the reload boost. In the second column, Explosive Payload is, again, probably best or Precision Instrument can be okay if you're hitting your headshots. Of the origin traits, Alacrity is best if you're running solo, in endgame activities to help clutch when everyone else is dead, or One Quiet Moment can be useful when in cover. Recommended MW: Stability, with a damage boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: The Prophet DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "The Prophet in PvP doesn’t really handle like a legendary DMT; more like a long ranged 120 hand cannon. Precision Instrument will make the 2c1b a little easier, but Kill Clip will be the most sought after perk for being able to 2c any resilience guardian. 120 hand cannons lost this ability so if you’re looking for a replacement, this is it." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: The Showrunner DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 78 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/5KOQQ9QtvSEhRVb4vjeO3B?si=8c4784e02b87424e) with @Saint_Kabr and @CourtProjects. First-choice PvE pick for add-clearing Minor Spec enemies in end-game content. The only other current lightweight SMG is Submission (which is a stat monster), so Showrunner's stats look a bit poor by comparison, but there are still some interesting PvE rolls worth considering. Lightweights give you +20 Mobility and +6.25% to movement speed as the frame bonus to start. You can push out the range with the barrel perk - Hammer Forged Rifling or Smallbore both work well, and Ricochet Rounds pushes out the range a bit further and adds a bit more stability. There are plenty of ways to proc the gunfeel boosts you get from Well Rounded and, once you've done that you can collect any generated elemental pickups to proc Collective Action (which is unusual to see on a Kinetic weapon) for a 20% damage boost. The Nadir Focus Origin Trait adding range and accuracy on repeated hits isn't game-changing, but it is nice to have. Recommended MW: Range, with a damage-boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-God, PvE-EndGame, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: The Showrunner DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "In PvP The Showrunner is going to be the least used SMG this season right behind Unending Tempest. Go see why I think that SMG is better in the Vendor breakdown. The Showrunner has some great perks in Target Lock and Dynamic Sway Reduction, Rangefinder if you're really looking for consistency at range, or more neutral perks like Well Rounded or Heating Up. However, the issue is really the frame we were given. Lightweight SMGs are not "in the spotlight." While I think this SMG can serve you well as a kinetic Funnelweb the perks to note aren't as strong. You can almost hit 100 range with Full Bore, Ricochet, Well Rounded 2x, and Fragile Focus, but that sacrifices Target Lock and Dynamic Sway Reduction for only 23m of range." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:controller,pvp

The Third Axiom DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @Saint_Kabr and @ImpetusAlways (PvE PvE-Utility PvE-Champion PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE roll for piercing Barrier Champions in activities rated Very Hard, Hard or Normal difficulty. The Third Axiom is an Adaptive pulse rifle which as a subfamily are currently rated C-Tier for endgame PvE by @TheAegisRelic Endgame Analysis spreadsheet in https://www.destiny2.science/ as "small step down from C tier pulse damage profile, but has barrier intrinsic". The frame intrinsic offers no bonuses beyond being "well-rounded, reliable and sturdy", as per the https://www.destiny2.science/ Destiny Data Compendium. The stats are on the low side by comparison with its peers - you can see the stats at https://destiny.report/w/3744058588/the-third-axiom and hopefully https://d2foundry.gg/w/3744058588 will show how weapon stats impact in-game performance when the site is back up and running. The recoil is a little left and a bit loose, so to tighten that up, look for a barrel perk that helps with Recoil Direction or, if you can handle it just go for extra handling, and then with the magazine perk go for Armor-Piercing Rounds for the additional ×1.05 shield damage, one of the other cambat effect mags or something that helps improve magazine size or reload. This is, admittedly, a niche roll but if you pair Genesis in the first trait perk column, to refill the mag after breaking a shield or Slideways to refill 15% of the mag and boost Stability by +15 and Handling by +30 (+18 and +33 when enhanced) when you slide, with Disruption Break in the second column to increase kinetic damage by ×1.5 for 5.5s after breaking an elemental shield or barrier, you should be able to quickly finish off Barrier Champions from a safe distance with a kinetic special weapon. The Vanguard Determination origin trait provides up to ×3 stacks that reduces incoming damage to max ×0.94 for a refreshable 10s (12.5s when enhanced), on kills within 20s of using a class ability. If you're interested, in mathematics, the "Third Axiom of Probability" (the axiom of countable additivity) dictates how separate, mutually exclusive events function - this ties perfectly into the weapon's flavour text. Recommended MW: Handling, with your preferred choice of mod to boost weapon stats, ammo generation, or elemental pickups for builds.|tags:PvE PvE-Utility PvE-Champion PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Tigerspite DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 91 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/50NPk7xF3Tt6X3DVucE9jU?si=44d2b7d44a5a4aec) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for add-clear of Minor Spec enemies in end-game content. Tigerspite has fairly middle-of-the-road stats and a more PvP-oriented trait pool but, if you like the subtype then there are a few interesting rolls. As with all precision weapon subtypes, you can start by not worrying too much about recoil direction, so you might as well push out the range with your barrel perk option. Any of the combat effect rounds in the mag perk slot (Armor-Piercing, High-Caliber or Ricochet Rounds) will add a tiny bit more range and utility in a firefight. In the first trait slot as the support perk, Subsistence really is the best (maybe only) trait that works for PvE while Frenzy is easy to proc and adds +15% damage, and 100 Handling and Reload, once it's procced. The Advanced Reflexes origin trait is quite useful in PvE - when activated by an enemy hit, it adds +30 Handling for additional ready and ADS speed, together with a handy +20 Mobility, for 7.5s. Recommended MW: Range or Stability, with a damage boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) mod. Tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Tigerspite DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Tigerspite is one of those guns that despite not having the best perks, the best stats, or even the best model; something about it feels phenomenal. I enjoy almost every Tigerspite I use, and I am excited to get more of them. Kinetic Tremors, Frenzy, or Kill Clip will depend on your activity and favorite damage boost. Pair it with your favorite reload or reload bypassing perk and you’ve got a dependable workhorse auto that just feels like bliss." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Tigerspite DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Tigerspite now has a better glow-up for PvP. Fragile Focus is great for starting an engagement but will quickly lose its value the longer you’re in a gunfight. I prefer Kill Clip in the final column to lower my TtK requirements in subsequent kills, but if you’re going to take this into Trials or 3v3 game modes, Eye of the Storm might be better for its consistency duel to duel." Recommened MW: Range.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Tripwire Canary DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB PvE Podcast 44. First-choice PvE roll for general content. Shapeable. The Ambush origin trait is probably more for PvP than PvE, with the extra range, handling and 10% boost to damage for a few moments after combat starts or the Veist Stinger will provide the occasional fast reload and draw. The Lightweight frame provides +20 mobility and 6.25% boost to movement, which is always useful. As with all bows, get the draw time as low as possible with Elastic String, and then recover the lost accuracy with Fiberglass Arrow Shaft. For end-game content, in the first trait column, Archer's Tempo rewards crit hits with even faster draw time. Explosive Head not only adds bonus damage passively, but also disrupts overload Champions in fewer hits, when the mod is around. Twilight Oath DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 91 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/50NPk7xF3Tt6X3DVucE9jU?si=44d2b7d44a5a4aec) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for sustained damage to Boss Spec enemies in end-game content. Twilight Oath starts in last place for stats amongst its peers, but it has one trick up its sleeve to make it useful for boss damage, if you don't already have a well-rolled special weapon for when you run out of heavy ammo. You'll need to start out by taming the wonky recoil with a Recoil Direction or Stability barrel perk. Tactical Mag, Appended Mag and Extended Mag all get you to the maximum of 7 shots - Tactical Mag adds some useful additional Stability too. You can use this with the trait combination of Envious Assassin, which can get 18 shots after 14 primary/heavy kills (and up to 21 if you reproc), together with Precision Instrument, which provides a scaling damage bonus of up to +25% damage on the sixth and every subsequent crit hit. The Advanced Reflexes origin trait is quite useful in PvE - when activated by an enemy hit, it adds +30 Handling for additional ready and ADS speed, together with a handy +20 Mobility, for 7.5s. Recommended MW: Stability, with a damage boosting (Boss or Taken Spec) mod. Tags: PvE, PvE-Boss, PvE-BossSpec, PvE-EndGame, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Tyranny of Heaven DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Draw Time. Lightweight Frame(580) Solar Combat Bow Primary Ammo.  "Tyranny of Heaven used to be the only solar bow in the game, and its sights are really odd. In its current version you can certainly get some good mileage out of the updated perks, only if you can agree with the wonky sights. Archer’s Tempo will get you the best reload / knock time, and Golden Tricorn is simply the best damage perk in the game. On solar that job gets even easier with the plethora of amazing abilities. Collective action will have slightly less uptime but could still be considered one of the better perks. Incandescent does give you some great AoE but I find that not bumping up the bow’s innate damage might feel a bit lackluster in higher end activities." |tags:pve,mkb,controller
