rolls.heading: Under Your Skin DIM Voltron recommendation
Unending Tempest DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 77 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/4RxkTOwLINWvKzUitNoAFy?si=1426d1f760c544b5) with @ImpetusAlways and @Saint_Kabr while @CourtProjects is on holiday. First-choice PvE pick for Minor Spec add clear enemies in end-game content. Unending Tempest is probably the best kinetic slot precision SMG, with great stats in comparison to its peers, and with being able to forget about the recoil direction with the frame bonus. But, as it's a Crucible weapon, there is a huge trait pool, so if you do want the best chance of a usable drop, reset your ranks a few times first, before you focus your engrams. We'll be using traits to boost handling and reload speed, so you can use the barrel and mag options to push up stability as much as you can to improve control. Working backwards, Frenzy as the signature perk maxes out handling and reload speed, as well as adding 15% to damage. It's always great to see Demolitionist in the first trait column as your support perk for more grenades, and who doesn't love that, and the occasional instant refill when you throw one. The Nadir Focus origin trait in may be more PvP orientated, but free extra range and accuracy is a nice bonus. Recommended MW: Stability, with a damage-boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) or Backup Mag mod.|tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-God, PvE-EndGame, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Unending Tempest DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Another season, another bajillion perks in our core weapons. Unending Tempest comes in as the first core playlist 600 rpm submachine gun. We have had Aggressives come from Crucible and Gambit. Lightweights from Crucible and Vanguard. So no Precision Frames. The biggest synergy is Demolitionist and Adrenaline Junkie. Enlightened Action and any damage bonus will also feel incredible. And of course there's Subsistence and Frenzy for damage and never having to reload." Recommended MW: Reload.|tags:controller,mkb,pve

rolls.heading: Unending Tempest DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Unending Tempest will be the next best submachine gun. Rivaling but not as strong as release The Immortal. The only issue is the large perk pool, but you can always work around that by playing crucible for more resets. The best perks depend on how you play. Killing Wind will help you get to and beat out your next engagement, Multikill Clip works here, but Kill Clip would have been better. Dynamic Sway Reduction will help keep your bullets on target paired with Target Lock for that quick TtK. Finally you could also try to hit max range with Fragile Focus and boost up your other stats with Perpetual Motion. It's definitely going to be a dominant SMG this season, and if you prefer precision SMGs like myself it will be worth the grind." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:controller,pvp

rolls.heading: Unexpected Resurgence DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Shield Duration. Adaptive Glaive(55) Arc Glaive Special Ammo.  "Truthfully I don’t know how to feel about the new glaive, it might need more time in my hands but it felt bad despite its beefed up stats. Thresh is great for getting super kills on both melee and powered shots. Subsistence for keeping the mag topped off or Feeding Frenzy for just reliable reload. I found the best success with Frenzy, but that’s due to it being a straightforward damage bonus, your mileage may vary. Prove me wrong with this glaive." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Unexpected Resurgence DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Range. Adaptive Glaive(55) Arc Glaive Special Ammo.  "In PvP glaive users might find a niche with adept mods being present on the first ever adept glaive. While there are at least 3 of you I might finally encounter a wild glaive in the crucible. If you are set on using glaives in PvP you do not need me to tell you what works and what doesn't." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Unforgiven DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S18] [MW: Reload] "Amazing SMG stinted by the (very) low Reload stat. If you can get lucky, and score Flared Magwell, Reload Masterwork and Frenzy, you'll have a very serviceable roll. Unfortunately, 100 full Duality runs later, and I don't have that 3/5 - so, good luck!" tags:pve|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Unforgiven DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB): "Unforgiven in PvP is a bit of a mixed bag. Aggressive frame submachine guns aren’t exactly the easiest to use on controllers, but MnK can really leverage its strengths. For both input methods, maxing out the recoil direction at 100 with either chambered compensator or extended barrel will be the first play. A good combination for grenade centered builds will be demolitionist and adrenaline junkie, but if you happen to find yourself on a more neutral void subclass then you might like golden tricorn. You probably won’t see golden tricorn 2x too often, but when you do and the stars align you’ll have a monster in your hands." Recommended MW - Range.|tags:pvp,mkb

rolls.heading: Until Its Return DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): Recommended MW: Reload Speed. Rapid-Fire Frame(140) Strand Shotgun Special Ammo.  "I am really excited for this shotgun. Cascade Point and your choice of either Overflow or Auto-Loading Holster for Fourth Horseman-lite. You can opt for any of the mag bonuses to hit cap in order to slot in a Major or Boss Spec mod. Other options include Collective Action for a small everything buff, or Vorpal Weapon to stack with your spec mod." |tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Until Its Return DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): Recommended MW: Handling. Rapid-Fire Frame(140) Strand Shotgun Special Ammo.  "In PvP you’re much better off using a different weapon as rapid-fire frames need two shots to reliably kill from a distance. If you are okay with that, then investing fully into handling and harmony for better damage after a kill would give you the best chance at competing with precision and aggressive frame shotguns." |tags:pvp,controller

rolls.heading: Unwavering Duty Perks and God Rolls
Veiled Threat Perks and God Rolls
Veleda-F DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Veleda-F is an interesting example of a sniper. It does one thing really well, and that's with the new perk Withering Gaze. It applies Weaken. Given any DPS rotation especially with Envious Arsenal, Recombination, Envious Assassin, etc. whatever you use this works perfectly with Bait and Switch. It applies Weaken for you and your allies. While not a great standalone sniper for end game activities." Recommended MW: Reload Speed.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Veleda-F DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Veleda-F is probably the only weapon I wanted to farm this season for a very easy 100 range and handling through barrels, MW and Slickdraw. Throw on Extended Mag and an Icarus grip for better AE and retire my Albruna-D. Being able to run anything in the fourth column is a nice bonus. BUT being a world drop nuked those chances. Closing Time is the nice new perk and can be paired with Lone Wolf as most people would grind for. If you're more old school you can go Snapshot and Opening Shot (as is tradition)." Recommended MW: Range or Handling.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Veles-X DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Veles-X is an aggressive burst pulse rifle that doesn’t need a raid to acquire. [...] If you happen to use void, however, getting golden tricorn 2x should be a cakewalk. The synergy is there to take this gun with echo of instability. Get a kill with Veles-X first, throw a grenade and get volatile rounds. You have golden tricorn 2x, this can stack with font of might for insane damage output. You have volatile rounds for add clear and if you get the kill you’ll have a small overshield to use. This is incredible for almost all activities. Unless you can’t get the grenade kill easily I would keep this in your inventory." Recommended MW - Stability.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Veles-X DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Veles-X will still have some incredible usefulness in PvP as well. Tunnel vision and kill clip are perfect with one another. At base you have an incredible pulse that can two burst. With kill clip it makes that two burst more forgiving but you also have tunnel vision for more aim assist. With the recent change to kill clip and tunnel vision, both can be refreshed so it keeps both perks active whenever you get a kill." Recommended MW - Stability.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Voltaic Shade Perks and God Rolls
Vouchsafe DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 91 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/50NPk7xF3Tt6X3DVucE9jU?si=44d2b7d44a5a4aec) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for add-clear of Minor Spec enemies in general content, particularly with a void subclass. The reprised Vouchsafe has returned with a plethora of PvE possibilities. You get the +20 Mobility from the Lightweight Frame, which is always great but on the downside you also have low Stability and Handling (not such a problem with the origin trait) and abysmal Recoil Direction. You can handle the wonky recoil with a combination of recoil and/or stability perks in the barrel and mag slots. You don't need a void subclass to make use of the trait combination of Repulsor Brace and Destabilizing Rounds - they'll work perfectly well without - but you can improve the effect with void fragments. The Advanced Reflexes origin trait is quite useful in PvE - when activated by an enemy hit, it adds +30 Handling for additional ready and ADS speed, together with a handy +20 Mobility, for 7.5s. Recommended MW: Stability, with a damage boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) mod. Tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-God, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Vouchsafe DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / Controller): "Lightweight frames in PvP are in an interesting spot. They don’t kill fast enough to be competitive, and any boosts don’t really elevate the weapon further. Golden Tricorn 2x does allow you to 3 shot any guardian at any resilience at a 0.60 TtK, but it requires two whole kills to get rolling and without additional ability kills you’ll be back at square one before you know it. Taraxippos and Eternal Blazon with Kill Clip or even Tarnished Mettle with Swashbuckler allow you to 3 crit after just one kill." Recommended MW: Stability.|tags:pvp,controller

rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S15] (PvE): [MW: Reload / Range] "Vulpecula is a stellar 180. Not only does it deal the extra 5% vs. shields due to its Elemental typing, it maintains the full Kinetic damage coding. In short, it is 5% stronger than every other 180RPM in PvE, blow-for-blow. As for stats, the Reload is a tad sluggish - invest into it, or rely on an Outlaw roll. Explosive Payload is always an S-Tier option, though seems to break Outlaw's registration sometimes - Headstone works as a kind of Stasis-crystal Dragonfly, which just feels great. This is going to be incredibly strong if Overload Hand Cannon becomes a Champion option again in future.|tags:god-pve

rolls.heading: Waking Vigil DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies Podcast vs Enemies episode 91 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/50NPk7xF3Tt6X3DVucE9jU?si=44d2b7d44a5a4aec) with @CourtProjects and @ImpetusAlways. First-choice PvE pick for add-clear of Minor Spec enemies in general content. As a former Lightweight HC that's been crowbarred into the Adaptive subfamily, the stats look very weird, with low range and stability but high handling and reload speed. If you haven't already got a decent arc hand cannon, then you could push out the range a bit, even at the cost of a little Handling, and add a little Stability. Any of the combat effect rounds in the mag perk slot (Armor-Piercing, High-Caliber or Ricochet Rounds) will add a tiny bit more range and utility in a firefight. Rapid Hit is the best support perk choice in the first column traits, although Perpetual Motion isn't bad either. Dragonfly in the second trait column as the signature perk is great for thinning out add crowds when you're hitting crits. The Advanced Reflexes origin trait is quite useful in PvE - when activated by an enemy hit, it adds +30 Handling for additional ready and ADS speed, together with a handy +20 Mobility, for 7.5s. Recommended MW: Range or Stability, with a damage boosting (Minor or Taken Spec) mod. Tags: PvE, PvE-MinorSpec, PvE-EndGame, M+KB, Controller

rolls.heading: Waking Vigil DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Let’s talk about power creep. When Waking Vigil released it was a phenomenal arc hand cannon. Outclassed by Nation of Beasts for the best arc hand cannon. With its midlife refresh Waking Vigil was blessed with a reload perk and an AoE perk through Dragonfly. However it was still overshadowed by Nation of Beasts and by then Ancient Gospel. When the hand cannon merger happened, Waking Vigil lost its only claim to fame being a Lightweight hand cannon versus the aforementioned adaptives. While we still have some great perks going on currently, Waking Vigil is just a relic from the past." Recommended MW: Handling.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Waking Vigil DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Waking Vigil used to be one of the most coveted energy hand cannons. A slimline model, access to Rangefinder and Opening Shot together to have an energy Dire Promise. Current form is a little less than stellar due to the lack of Slideshot, but it still retains the OG roll if you still care about Rangefinder." Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Wastelander M5 DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 279 with @Saint_Kabr. First-choice PvP pick for duelling. Shapeable. The Hot Swap origin trait works well for PvP, boosting handling when swapping to the weapon while damaged for quick swaps and ADS speed. The Lightweight frame suits aggressive, mobile playstyles, boosting sprint speed by 6.25% and passively adding 20 mobility to help close gaps quickly - you'll need to hit 11 out of 12 pellets for a kill at any resilience. Good all-round stats within the subfamily. For Lightweights, range needs to be over 80 to give a 6.28m range - aim to get to 55 with your barrel, mag and masterwork, without hurting handling or projectile spread. Then Slideshot will add 20, and Opening Shot another 25 to get to 100, which pushes the range out to 7m. Just remember to ABS (always be sliding), and always follow up with a melee! Recommended MW: Range, with a Targeting Adjustor, Quick Access Sling or Sprint Grip mod depending on your preference.|tags: PvP, PvP-Duel, PvP-God, MKB, Controller

rolls.heading: Best Wendigo GL3 Rolls for PvE and PvP
Whispering Slab DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvE / M+KB / Controller): "Whispering Slab is a fan favorite bow. It has been a while since it saw usage as it got power crept pretty rapidly. Does it hold up now? Archer's Tempo and Vorpal Weapon are nothing flashy but it's honest work. Then you have the options of Swashbuckler and Pugilist or Adrenaline Junkie and Demolitionist. Depending on your build it could fit nicely. It's not anything crazy as a re-release, but most copies of the original aren't as good." Recommended MW: Draw Time.|tags:pve,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Whispering Slab DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Lightweight Bows in PvP have not had a good footing starting from the health changes Bungie made. Opening Shot almost guarantees a crit and add in the bonuses from Lone Wolf and you might have a good solo weapon. It still doesn't feel incredible to miss a shot, so all these bonuses will hopefully make this bow feel adequate to use." Recommended MW: Draw Time.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Whispering Slab DIM Voltron recommendation
rolls.notes: (PvP): Mercules904 (OK PvP) - "A slightly faster draw time does not make up for how much less damage they do, which makes them harder to use in both PvP and PvE (even with the 10% damage buff to red bars from the most recent sandbox patch). They're less likely to kill injured enemies in PvP, and their accuracy (which is basically the range stat for bows) is really atrocious which means you're a lot more likely to find enemies outside of your optimal engagement range than if you use a Precision bow, Trinity Ghoul, or Le Monarque. That being said, they do have some benefits to them statistically, like high aim assist within their optimal range and a snappy feeling thanks to quick reload speed and handling."|tags:pvp

rolls.heading: Wolftone Draw DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S15] (PvE): [MW: Draw Time / Reload Speed] "Wolftone Draw is special. Currently, its the only Precision Frame Bow that can roll with Impulse Amplifier, which massively speeds up your time between shots via projectile speed and reload time. There's a lot that works with this Bow, but it being able to sport Dragonfly with a higher damage-per-arrow really lets it leave Arsenic Bite behind. Treat this thing like a baby Trinity Ghoul, and it will do right by you. Some fantastic synergy with Archer's Tempo and Adagio too, which translates to being able to one-shot heavier PvE red-bars in most activities while maintaining a lightning-fast Draw Time. No Explosive Head on this one however, which is incredibly helpful for Overload Champions."|tags:pve

rolls.notes: pandapaxxy (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Yarovit MG4 has the makings to become the best Stasis smg in the game. Over in the crucible it’s only slightly different. Yarovit MG4 has the best consistency perks in Zen Moment and Dynamic Sway Reduction. It doesn’t matter what way you slice it, this weapon just will not move. This is before taking into consideration Omolon Fluid Dynamics allowing you to full send into range as your stability is covered. If you want even more stability out of the gate, Strategist bumps up your stability after you use your class ability and kills with this weapon add class ability energy. Headstone will be better with Stasis synergy but also denies revives with the crystal in game modes that allow revives/" Recommended MW: Range.|tags:pvp,mkb,controller

rolls.heading: Yesteryear DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: [YeezyGT's S18] [MW: Handling] "Yesteryear is a gun with a dozen great options... on paper. You'll know which people have actually used the gun by the recommendations / rolls they're chasing. The base recoil direction and stability combo is bad. It kicks, 50RD is inconsistent at best, and sabotaging your play at worst. But -- if you get Heating Up, it completely fixes this, and allows you to make use of the utterly fantastic fourth column perks without reprieve. Heating Up / Golden Tricorn is my number 1, followed by Slideways, then more traditional options like Outlaw / Rampage, etc. If you're not running Heating Up, then definitely go for Arrowhead Brake and even consider running Counterbalance Stock here. Consistency is king." tags:pve|tags:pve

rolls.heading: Yesteryear DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by DMB Podcast 278 with @Critbuff. First-choice PvP pick for duelling. As with all playlist weapons, the more reputation resets you can get, the more trait options you'll get to play with, otherwise you've only got a 1/144 chance of any pair of traits dropping, before you even look at barrel, mag and masterwork. You have access to the best PvP Origin Perk in Suros Synergy in a solid Pulse Rifle subfamily, needing only the smallest of damage buffs to go from a TTK of 0.93s with the first bullet of a third burst (6 crits, 1 body), to a 0.6s six crit kill. Stats are on the low side, including a truly terrible Recoil Direction of 50, so you will need a Counterbalance mod even with Arrowhead Brake, but you'll also benefit from its reduction of 30 bounce intensity, so it's still the best barrel park. With the mag, Ricochet Rounds or Flared Magwell round out the stats, adding some needed Stability. For a duelling roll, look for Perpetual Motion and Eye of the Storm to keep your reticule on target and make your crits easier to hit in a gunfight. Recommended MW: Stability, with a Counterbalance mod.|tags: PvP, PvP-Duel, PvP-God, MKB, Controller

rolls.heading: Zaouli’s Bane Perks and God Rolls
Zealous Ideal Perks and God Rolls
Zephyr DIM Voltron PVE recommendation
rolls.notes: Inspired by @PodvsEnemies episode 126 from 17 December 2024 (https://open.spotify.com/episode/6SVRlV4ttvPkAm9HA44Km0?si=a9d7a25536b747c3) with @ImpetusAlways, @CourtProjects and @Saint_Kabr (PvE PvE-Boss PvE-BossSpec PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller): First-choice PvE pick for boss damage in general, and end-game underleveled content. Zephyr isn't quite as unique as it was when it appeared as the first stasis sword but it's still a useful addition to a vault for CQC boss encounters. All swords have pretty much the same base stats - except Bequest with its 70 Impact - so this stacks up decently by comparison. With 0/1/2/3 Reserves mods, you'll have total ammo of 67/71/73/74 (max 77). Choose a blade perk that boosts damage, and then the usual play is Swordmaster's Guard as the guard perk to speed up heavy attack regeneration time for maximum damage. In the first trait perk column, Relentless Strikes returns one ammo for every three hits, while in the second Whirlwind Blade boosts damage by up to ×1.3 at 10 stacks, or if you're in a crowd Surrounded boosts ×1.3 (×1.4175 when enhanced) without any need for stacking. The Dawning Surprise origin trait is pretty potent for PvE (although it won't proc much during boss damage) - 6 Minor add rapid kills drops a "gift" for your fireteam which on pickup grants +20% energy to all your abilities, restores 22HP and starts health regeneration. Recommended MW: Impact, with a Backup Mag mod.|tags:PvE PvE-Boss PvE-BossSpec PvE-EndGame PvE-God M+KB Controller

rolls.heading: Zephyr DIM Voltron PVP recommendation
rolls.notes: Panda (PvP / M+KB / Controller): "Tireless Blade will get you one ammo back after every other kill, and swords OHK. The extra damage from Chain Reaction will make follow up kills easier or just get you some fun multikills." Recommended MW: Impact.|tags:pvp,controller,mkb
